programme.desc: DHL Stormers v British & Irish Lions. Extended highlights of the latest match on the tour, held at Cape Town Stadium. With Lauren Jenkins, Gareth Charles and Gwyn Jones

programme.title: Sgwrs Dan y Lloer
programme.desc: Elin Fflur chats to Trystan Ellis-Morris in his garden, finding out about his career and the sort of person he is at home

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the case of bookmaker John Dickman, who, in 1910, became the last man to be hanged at Newcastle Prison. Bookman was found guilty of the murder of John Nisbet, who was robbed and shot five times

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dr Sam Ma has a chest pain mystery to solve when a ship's electrician is flown to Shetland for emergency treatment - but is it a heart condition or something else? Dr Lalla mentors Dr Sam Potter as she diagnoses a Lerwick man's skin complaints

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Jack gets nearer to finding the other missing boy, and the hunt for Eddie's attacker compromises Pratt's relationship with his son. Meanwhile, there is another rape, but this time the consequences are deadly, and Gina and Jack become closer following some trouble at home

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £450,000, a married couple looking to uproot from Greater Manchester seek the help of Jules Hudson to find a new family home close to the coast in Dorset. Jules is in his element when he meets one of the county's blacksmiths, who specialises in restoring relics of bygone eras using traditional techniques

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team head to Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie Balmer gambles on some furniture, while Charlie Ross is excited to find a rare antique he has never seen before. Who will make the most profit at auction in Sidmouth?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the hidden history of the nurses, doctors and health workers who, for more than 70 years, have been arriving from overseas to serve in the NHS. Without them the NHS would have been in danger of collapse - not least during the current Covid-19 crisis. From its origins, the story of this beloved institution has been intertwined with one of the most divisive social and political issue of the age - immigration - and those who came to work in the NHS have found themselves fighting battles of hostility they neither sought nor expected. However, as Olusoga reveals, their stories say as much about perseverance, dedication and the overcoming of obstacles as they do about discrimination and prejudice

programme.title: Secrets of an Isis Smartphone
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar gains access to the contents of a smartphone used by three British men who left their homes to go to Syria and fight with Isis. Tthe reporter tries to understand their motivations, how they became radicalised and ultimately who is responsible for their one-way journey to certain death. Using never-before-seen videos, photos and screenshots from the device, Mobeen explores what made the trio join an estimated 900 Britons who went out to Syria and Iraq to fight with the world's most feared terrorist group

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine whether a young mother accused of poisoning her family in 1850 was hanged as a result of hearsay and gossip. Sarah Chesham had gained a reputation of a serial poisoner three years earlier when she was accused of killing three local boys using poison. She was acquitted on all charges, but the shadow of these grisly events hung over her. Ordered to stand trial for the attempted murder of her husband Richard - despite little evidence against her - Sarah, with no lawyer acting in her defence, was swiftly found guilty

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks at some of the most interesting animal-related items bought by past contestants and also heads to Peterborough to discover an extraordinary collection of horseshoes

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina's life is placed in danger when an attempt to lure the killer into a trap takes a deadly turn, while Jack makes a horrifying discovery about the criminal that could prove vital to his colleague's survival. Crime drama, starring Jennie Jacques and Kieran Bew

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers remember some of their favourite exotic garden rescues, featuring spaces that invited influence from far-flung countries all over the world. They include a Zambian-inspired makeover for a couple in Watford, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Newcastle who love their Lake District holidays so much they want to make the area their permanent home. With a budget of £750,000, they are looking for a property with fabulous views and enough space for visiting family. Alistair also takes to the water when he meets a passionate boat builder who restored what is thought to be the oldest passenger ferry in the world

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to Somerset to work with Vicki and Bryan, who dream of spending less time at work and more time with their young family by turning a corner of their field into a unique B&B. They have a space-age geodome, but have no idea how to transform it into a welcoming rental. Simon and his team are full of ideas, including a large wooden statement bed and set of bunks, plus a botanical theme to bring warmth and colour to the large space. It's a big and physically challenging job, but with help from friends and family the team hopes to pull off an extraordinary transformation

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy get a family pet in an attempt to teach the children to be more responsible. But as ever, things do not quite work out as planned. Domestic comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance from former EastEnders actress Annette Badland (Aunt Babe)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Billy sets up his stall, Rocky passes by and challenges him to a head-to-head battle to see who can sell the most in 10 minutes. Gray's boss Laura makes it clear she is unhappy with his performance - so he takes matters into his own hands. Suki is angry to find Honey running the Minute Mart in her absence

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A hen party on the island ends in tragedy when the bride-to-be is found dead in her bath the following morning. It doesn't take long for Humphrey to work out she was murdered - and when the electronic key proves she was the last person to open the door, it looks like she knew her killer. But why was the body in the bath? Meanwhile, Camille has a big decision to make. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring Amy Nuttall, William Ash and Ali Bastian

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Meera Sodha plus special guest Miles Jupp. Helen McGinn joins via video link and is picking the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a range of recipes to tempt family and friends, featuring classics, simple British dishes and modern favourites. In the first edition, Mary indulges her love of comfort food with heartwarming recipes including eggs Benedict Florentine, slow-cooked lamb shanks, and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots, before enjoying open-fire cooking with Swedish chef Niklas Esktedt

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent, and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Glamorgan are all sold at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Richard Madley helps two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Castleford Tigers (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. The Saints have defeated holders Leeds Rhinos, Huddersfield Giants and Hull FC to reach this stage and will, perhaps surprisingly given their recent success in the Super League, be looking to lift this trophy for the first since 2008. The Tigers have overcome Hull Kingston Rovers, Salford Red Devils and Warrington Wolves so far and are now aiming to end a 35-year wait to win the cup

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees a married couple from Worcester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win the money they need to get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha must decide where her heart lies, and a patient awaiting a kidney transplant from a friend deliberately sabotages his chances of a successful operation. When Ollie falls ill at school, David is plunged into a paranoid spiral as he suspects Rosa is still trying to harm Ollie - but when the boy falls ill at school, he begins to consider other possibilities

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German Army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein look back at the poisoning of a man in 1930s Lincolnshire, for which his wife was found guilty. They meet a relative of the alleged killer who is eager to find out the truth

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant help two teams at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, while Charlie Ross finds out about a collection of medieval church paintings

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: Brian explores the enigma of time, a phenomenon that is taken for granted but which, as far as physicists are concerned, is one of the biggest mysteries in the universe. He recalls highlights from his TV series that touch upon different sides of this physics conundrum, from breaking the sound barrier in a Eurofighter in order to see two sunsets in one day, to witnessing one of the oldest animal life cycles on Earth on a remote beach in Costa Rica

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Staffordshire, Vanessa Elliot from London takes on Rukun Ahmed from Yorkshire. They buy items at auction in the hope they can sell them and make a profit. Paul Martin presents

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines clues hidden in the landscape to reveal how the Americas were formed from an ancient supercontinent, later named Pangaea. He finds evidence that 300 million years ago New York was at the heart of a huge mountain range, and travels to an extinct volcano in Bolivia to explore subduction - the process that has shaped South America

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland chefs prepare their seafood courses as they try to win a place at the Taste of Summer banquet honouring 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. Joery Castel combines mackerel and cucumber in his dish, Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andy Murray's apparent love of sushi and Eddie Attwell creates five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his effort

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing Northern Ireland, Tommy Heaney, Joery Castel and Eddie Attwell, go all out to impress with their main courses. Barbecued dishes are on the menu for two of the chefs, with Tommy creating a surf and turf combination, while Joery aims to recreate a more American style barbecue, and Eddie opts for an unusual meat, water buffalo, for the centrepiece of his main course

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels to the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where he realises a lifelong dream by spending time with Grauer's gorillas, the largest primates on earth. He meets the fearsome alpha male Chimanuka and two babies, one a lonely orphan and as Gordon gains the trust of these mysterious, seldom seen animals, he discovers their incredible compassion and gets caught up in a battle between giant rival silverbacks for control of the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to Dover Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin looks at the life of James Bond creator Ian Fleming

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty, Jordan Stephens, Sally Lindsay and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A tiger undergoes surgery, but the vets are soon faced with a difficult decision. A cockatoo ruffles some feathers, and the park's white rhinos enjoy a detoxing charcoal spa

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Monty travels to Japan in spring to explore different aspects of the nation's gardens. Amidst the cherry blossoms, he begins his journey by visiting the earliest surviving boating garden of the Heian period. He also looks at the rolling green moss of a Buddhist garden and learns the secrets of creating a Zen landscape before visiting an unconventional garden created by a modern design legend. The presenter also learns about the art of Japanese stonemasonry, the famous tea ceremonies and receives a lesson in the delicate art of traditional flower arranging

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the first day of the Major, taking place at Royal St. George's Golf Club in Sandwich, Kent. The tournament was due to be played at this venue last year for the first time since Darren Clarke prevailed in 2011. However, it was cancelled due to the Covid-19 pandemic, meaning Shane Lowry enters as the defending champion, having taken the honours at Portrush in 2019

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show, featuring a different topic each week. Here, comedians Jason Byrne, Marlon Davis, Micky Flanagan, Jessica Fostekew, Guz Khan, Sean Lock and Sindhu Vee perform routines about children

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Forensic scene investigator Drew Birtles searches for evidence when a woman in her 50s alleges that an engineer working in her home indecently exposed himself. Facing a case that is difficult to prove, Drew sets to work using forensic light sources and DNA evidence to build a picture of what went on in the woman's house.

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: Desperate for success and tempted by the lucrative rewards brought by towing the line, Jimmy White joined Barry Hearn in the hope he could be turned into a champion like his new stablemate Steve Davis. With White as the hot-favourite and with the crowd urging him on against 21-year-old Stephen Hendry, the final of 1990 world championship turned out to be a pivotal moment in British snooker and paved the way for what was to come in the next decade

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Ben and Myles visit an abandoned hotel in search of money owed to a former employee, and the Sheriffs come up against a car dealer who is refusing to refund a customer

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the case of a servant, accused of murdering his upper-class benefactor in Devon in 1884, who made history as 'The Man They Could Not Hang'

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the case of bookmaker John Dickman, who, in 1910, became the last man to be hanged at Newcastle Prison. Bookman was found guilty of the murder of John Nisbet, who was robbed and shot five times

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: The Great Northern Garden Build
programme.desc: Documentary following the creation of RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford, one of the most ambitious and challenging gardening projects the UK has ever seen. The programme goes behind the scenes to meet the team of designers, engineers and gardeners taking on this colossal construction project on the site of a former grand Victorian estate in Greater Manchester

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Contestants from Birmingham and London take part in the antiques challenge, presented by Paul Martin. The pair are shown the tricks of the trade before an auction showdown

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines key moments in the development of Eurasia, which is the combined continental land mass of Europe and Asia and was formed by a series of tectonic-plate collisions over millions of years. In Kerala, a freshwater fish provides evidence that Madagascar was once joined to India, which created the Himalayas as it pushed its way 4,000km north to its present position

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Northern Ireland chefs Eddie Attwell, Tommy Heaney and Joery Castel prepare their desserts, knowing this is the last chance to impress before one of them has to leave the competition. Two of them attempt technical dishes featuring edible chocolate tennis balls, while the third cooks a traditional Dutch pancake. Which two will make it through to tomorrow?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland cook for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver, who are joined by tennis player Mansour Bahrami as guest judge. Only one contender can be chosen to represent the region in the national finals, taking them a step closer to cooking at the Wimbledon Championships

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to the Democratic Republic of Congo in the hope of finding the gorilla family again, and discovering the outcome of silverback Chimanuka's confrontation with a rival. He soon finds the apes, but discovers their lives are now under threat from poachers, and works with the local rangers to prevent them coming to harm

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts James Lewis and Catherine Southon search for items to take off to auction at Dover Castle in Kent. James unearths a silver owl inkwell and Catherine discovers an alabaster bust. Presenter Paul Martin delves into the history of the English Channel

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Newsreader Naga Munchetty, actor-singer Jordan Stephens, actress Sally Lindsay and funnyman David O'Doherty continue the daily test of their skills, hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble learn of the efforts of Longleat's keepers in the Australian bushfire animal rescue, and find out how to assess the health of an elephant from its poo

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Part two of two. The horticultural enthusiast explores Japan during autumn. He begins as he did in the spring, at one of the 'three great gardens of Japan' to learn how they are protected from the coming winter. Monty follows the history of the Japanese garden through the military strongholds of past leaders to the many styles, new and old, of the iconic stroll garden and finds a slice of Victorian England in the heart of Tokyo. He also learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle. He ends his journey by the Sea of Japan at a place that makes art out of its gardens

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the second day of the Open Championship, taking place at Royal St. George's Golf Club in Sandwich, Kent. JB Holmes and eventual champion Shane Lowry shared the lead on eight-under at this stage in Portrush when the tournament was last held two years ago. At the other end of the leaderboard, both Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson missed the cut, the first time both of them had done so in 83 Majors, while other notable casualties included home player Graeme McDowell and defending champion Francesco Molinari

programme.title: I'll Get This
programme.desc: An eclectic mix of five celebrities go out for dinner together. When they arrive at the restaurant, they must place their bank card in the centre of the table. As the food is served and the drinks flow, they play a series of fun and revealing games, and if they win a game, they can retrieve their card. The person whose card remains at the end of the evening must pick up the bill for the whole group. Taking part in the first edition are Rylan Clark-Neal, Ed Gamble, Carol Vorderman, Ellie Taylor and Anton Du Beke

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the first T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Trent Bridge, Nottingham

programme.title: Goldstone (2016)
programme.desc: Detective Jay Swan's investigation of a young woman's disappearance causes friction in an Outback township under a mining company's sway. Local cop Josh Waters is growing used to looking the other way over everyday corruption, while Aboriginal elder Jimmy sees Jay has a deeper link to the territory. Thriller, starring Aaron Pedersen, Alex Russell, David Wenham and David Gulpill

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC correspondent Lucy Adams is one of more than a million people in the UK with Long Covid. In his programme, she reports on what the future holds for sufferers of long Covid symptoms, talking to patients, doctors with increasing caseloads, and scientists carrying out research into the condition

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies encounter a digital entrepreneur passionate about the potential of her online hypno-birthing platforms and two north-east business partners with a unique recycled building system. A dynamic pair of jewellers charm the potential investors with their bespoke ring-making kits, while another hopeful anticipates the Dragons will get stuck into his plant-based, biodegradable chewing gum. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Strange places where animals can set up home, including penguins nesting in a garden, pigs swimming in the sea, and a fish living inside the bottom of a sea cucumber

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Tali wants a den where she can practice her magic tricks in secret, without giving the game away

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Two sisters plan to put a very 'on-trend' wedding for their mother and soon to be step-dad with a fashion-themed big day. With the help of Instagram sensation, make-up expert and Zayn Malik's sister, Doniya Malik, the girls put on a fashion show like you've never seen before, complete with a real catwalk fashion show

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey makes her last appearance as a presenter on the show

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari is creating his family's favourite snack - choccy oat bars, which are filled with oats, syrup and chocolate and are the perfect treat

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve's on a mission to find dinosaurs that have something a little special - fearsome killers, with incredible abilities. His quest sees him race in the dark like a Troodon, track prey from miles away taking tips from the Daspletosaurus, and finally gets his hands on a real life Caiman to show how their pressure pits are just like that of the Spinosaurus

programme.title: Orbit: Earth's Extraordinary Journey
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Dr Helen Czerski examine Earth's annual trip around the sun to show the effect it has on the planet, including its influence on the weather and the tides. The first episode covers the period from July to the winter solstice in December, with Helen leaping from an aeroplane and Kate briefly becoming the fastest driver in the world to demonstrate the consequences of the orbit on Earth's inhabitants

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: This episode looks at life on the highest mountain range on Earth, the Himalayas, home to many extraordinary animals and remarkable ancient cultures. The programme follows snow leopards as they creep into isolated mountain villages searching for food, and monkeys huddling for warmth in a hidden, frozen forest, and the athletes who compete in the gruelling Everest marathon

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The series turns to animals that fly, including footage of half a million mother bats, an agile sparrowhawk raiding for food, and large beetles taking to the sky as part of their mating ritual. The programme also reveals how peregrine falcons are able to reach speeds of 200mph, and why an albatross's nose helps it to fly. Narrated by Suranne Jones

programme.title: Nadiya's Party Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite festive dishes, making the perfect food to hand around and share, such as melt-in-the-mouth halloumi chips, smoked mackerel pate choux and sticky lamb ribs. She also prepares a hearty dive-in dish of beef chilli and jalapeno corn bread and some vibrant salads - Brussels sprout slaw and pomegranate and parsley tabbouleh. Plus, for dessert a chocolate eclair roll and passion fruit bundt. The accomplished cook also joins a group of children as they make cupcakes, lends a hand with a trifle and helps out with a surprise birthday cake

programme.title: Nature's Miracle Orphans
programme.desc: Patrick Aryee visits a rescue centre in Costa Rica where an anteater is learning to fend for itself, and Lucy Cooke meets an elephant in Zimbabwe with a fear of water

programme.title: The Wedding Planner (2001)
programme.desc: San Francisco wedding planner Mary Fiore organises other people's nuptials but has never had much luck in love. One day she is swept off her feet by handsome doctor Steve Edison. They spend a magical night together and all seems perfect, but her dreams of him becoming her husband suffer a setback when she discovers she has already been hired to organise his marriage to another woman. Will true love find a way? Director Adam Shankman's romantic comedy, starring Jennifer Lopez, Matthew McConaughey, Bridgette Wilson-Sampras, Justin Chambers and Joanna Gleason

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters and crew reflect on memorable visits to India, Bhutan and Bangladesh, with Liz Bonnin remembering a remarkable morning migration by thousands of cranes to the small village of Khichan in Rajasthan. Chris Packham looking back on a tiger-spotting trip, while Steve Backshall and Gordon Buchanan recall an expedition looking for tigers in the mountains of Bhutan

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer and actor Jordan Stephens, presenter and actor Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery

programme.title: Dad's Army (1971)
programme.desc: The ragtag soldiers of the Walmington-on-Sea Home Guard are given the chance to prove their mettle by apprehending three German aviators who have parachuted into the country in the vanguard of a suspected Nazi invasion. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier, Clive Dunn, Bill Pertwee, Ian Lavender and Arnold Ridley

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the Open Championship, taking place at Royal St. George's Golf Club in Sandwich, Kent. Shane Lowry held a four-stroke lead over Tommy Fleetwood at the end of this stage when the tournament was last held two years ago in Portrush, breaking the course record with an eight-under-par 63

programme.title: Liam Gallagher: As It Was (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about the former Oasis frontman. The film by directors Charlie Lightening and Gavin Fitzgerald follows his journey, from the dizzying heights of superstardom with his brother Noel in the 1990s, to years in the musical wilderness, before his hugely successful comeback as a solo artist in recent years

programme.title: Britpop at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at the vibrant form of indie music known as Britpop, which in the 1990s stamped its presence onto the UK cultural scene and led to the notion of 'Cool Britannia', as a new wave of bands re-evaluated what it meant to be British. The movement was dominated by the battle between its biggest-selling exponents, Blur and Oasis, who in August 1995 did battle in a memorable bid for the number one spot. The programme also features the bands Elastica, Sleeper, Suede and Menswear

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A hot summer swimming spot is the latest painting to take shape under the brush of American artist Bob Ross

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame, revealing that there was plenty of real-life drama and heartbreak aboard the luxury ship

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: The Importance of Being Oscar
programme.desc: A star-studded documentary-drama providing an account of Oscar Wilde's glittering career before his trial for homosexual crimes and his tragic fall from grace. Wilde enthusiasts and experts such Stephen Fry and Wilde's grandson Merlin Holland provide revelatory accounts of how the writer's own life informed his work, intercut with adaptations of his comedies and books performed by a cast including Freddie Fox, Claire Skinner, Anna Chancellor and James Fleet

programme.title: Wilde (1997)
programme.desc: Biopic of author and acclaimed wit Oscar Wilde, focusing on his affair with Lord Alfred Douglas at a time when homosexual liaisons were illegal. Refusing to flee the country when their relationship was uncovered, he was subsequently jailed, enduring two years of hard labour. Starring Stephen Fry, Jude Law, Vanessa Redgrave and Jennifer Ehle

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: Jeanne, Simon and Gemma enter the mountain, uncovering traces of past life. On Ulysses, Doisneau and Wayne have the crew at their mercy. In French, Russian, English and German

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: Beyond the cavern, Jeanne learns more of what is required of her, and becomes the Z2 crew's prime target. On Ulysses, desperate measures take a terrible toll. In French

programme.title: Missions
programme.desc: The survivors race for Zillion 2, but neither Gemma nor Goldstein, her boss on Earth, have rescue in mind. In French

programme.title: Return to The Cruise
programme.desc: Jane McDonald and the crew continue their look back at the influential fly-on-the-wall documentary that shot them to fame, revealing that there was plenty of real-life drama and heartbreak aboard the luxury ship

programme.title: Tails You Win: The Science of Chance
programme.desc: Professor David Spiegelhalter tries to get to grips with chance and how it works. He tackles such questions as whether people are wise to take an umbrella when they go out, to ways of maximising the chances of living to 100, as he tries to establish if luck and fate are embedded in the fabric of the universe. Along the way, he discovers hidden patterns and shapes in a book written exclusively with numbers and explains why going sky-diving only marginally increases his risk of dying sooner rather than later

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The Grammy award-winning soul and jazz star examines the sound and work of his favourite vocal showstoppers, starting with the world's first pop star, the Italian tenor Enrico Caruso. He moves on to admire the genius of Ella Fitzgerald, the soul of Mahalia Jackson, the power and stagecraft of Freddie Mercury, the artistry and extravagance of Prince, and the modern melisma of Whitney Houston and Mariah Carey. Contributors include Dave Grohl, Beck, Adam Lambert, George Benson, Stephen Costello, Wendy Melvoin and Lisa Coleman

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first aired on April 25 1991, featuring performances by EMF, De La Soul, Vic Reeves and the Roman Numerals, Electronic, Blur, Gloria Estefan, Chesney Hawkes and Quadrophonia

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo presents the edition first aired on May 2 1991, featuring performances by the KLF, Cathy Dennis, Orchestral Manoeuvres in the Dark, Samantha Janus, Nomad, Zucchero and Paul Young, Frances Nero, Cher and Roachford

programme.title: Eric Clapton: Life in 12 Bars
programme.desc: Documentary profiling the blues guitarist, narrated by the man himself, as he charts his six decades in the music business, and why his insatiable desire to grow his artistic voice led him to quit a stream of successful bands, from the Yardbirds and John Mayall and the Bluesbreakers to short-lived supergroups Cream and Blind Faith. Clapton also talks about his battles with drugs and alcohol, his love for George Harrison's wife Pattie Boyd and the tragic loss of his son in an accident. Including archive interviews with BB King, George Harrison and Jimi Hendrix

programme.title: Eric Clapton at the BBC: The Rock 'n' Roll Years
programme.desc: Archive performances by the guitarist and songwriter, who has survived both personal trauma and drug abuse to build a wide and varied musical career. He began with R&B group the Yardbirds, before moving on to join John Mayall and the Bluesbreakers. He also collaborated with Ginger Baker and Jack Bruce in Cream, and worked with Steve Winwood and John Lennon. He now enjoys success as a solo performer, and is often acclaimed as one of the greatest guitarists of his generation

programme.title: Great Guitar Riffs at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of archive clips and performances which include some of the most memorable guitar riffs of all time. Featuring Jimi Hendrix, the Kinks, Cream, AC/DC, the Smiths, Rage Against the Machine, Radiohead, Foo Fighters, Pixies and the Stone Roses

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Status Quo play Pictures of Matchstick Men on Top of the Pops in 1968, the Who perform Long Live Rock in the Old Grey Whistle Test studio, Dire Straits deliver Tunnel of Love and Lynyrd Skynyrd bring a taste of the American South with Sweet Home Alabama. The programme also features rare performances from George Benson, Leo Kottke, Link Wray and Tom Petty

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first aired on April 25 1991, featuring performances by EMF, De La Soul, Vic Reeves and the Roman Numerals, Electronic, Blur, Gloria Estefan, Chesney Hawkes and Quadrophonia

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo presents the edition first aired on May 2 1991, featuring performances by the KLF, Cathy Dennis, Orchestral Manoeuvres in the Dark, Samantha Janus, Nomad, Zucchero and Paul Young, Frances Nero, Cher and Roachford

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The Grammy award-winning soul and jazz star examines the sound and work of his favourite vocal showstoppers, starting with the world's first pop star, the Italian tenor Enrico Caruso. He moves on to admire the genius of Ella Fitzgerald, the soul of Mahalia Jackson, the power and stagecraft of Freddie Mercury, the artistry and extravagance of Prince, and the modern melisma of Whitney Houston and Mariah Carey. Contributors include Dave Grohl, Beck, Adam Lambert, George Benson, Stephen Costello, Wendy Melvoin and Lisa Coleman

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke focuses on the reasons behind the revolution that gave birth to classical Greek art. He travels in Sicily to the Valley of the Temples in Agrigento before heading to the island of Mozia to see the Charioteer, which was unearthed in 1979. He also marvels at the athletic bodies of the Riace bronzes - warriors dragged from the seabed - as he asks how the Greeks became so good so quickly

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop continue their comprehensive guide to Britain's coastline, this time turning attention to the North Sea coast, a stretch that takes in two nations, and is bordered by a pair of huge estuaries, the Forth in Scotland, and the Humber in England. Neil sheds light on a military shipbuilding project in Rosyth, and heads to the island of Inchmickery to investigate a bird colony with a difference. From there, he tells a tragic story that still haunts the fishing village of Eyemouth, and then visits the holy island of Lindisfarne to make a dramatic archeological discovery

programme.title: Beck
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a Nobel Peace Prize candidate. Detective drama, starring Peter Haber as Inspector Martin Beck. In Swedish

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter focuses on the three weeks leading up to Ruth Ellis's execution, and the last-minute attempts to save her life even though Ruth herself was determined to die. The killer finally admitted she had an accomplice, prompting her solicitor to race to the Home Office in a bid to stop the execution. But it was all in vain and Ruth was hanged. Gillian goes on to explore the role of her case in the introduction of the defence of diminished responsibility in England and its place in the eventual abolition of capital punishment in 1965

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Robert and Maja harbour hopes that Emilie may still be alive and Zeeland throw all their resources into tracking her down, while a secret is revealed from an unlikely source within the family. Borch and Lund have a hard time shaking off their Jutland experience, with their working relationship suffering as a result, and police run checks on Ussing's movements over the past couple of years. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Robert intensifies the search for his daughter, despite coming under heavy pressure from the Zeeland board, while the prime minister and his close aides find themselves the focus of police attention as Karen hides a dangerous secret. Borch and Lund's trip to Jutland begins to have consequences, and a witness turns up who may have important information about Emilie. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Sarah is held back in her attempts to make headway with the investigation, while the perpetrator finally has a face. Elsewhere, prime minister Kamper must clean up his circle as he tries to find out what happened to his son. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: The election has finally arrived and the prime minister has everything to gain, while the police are led to an undisclosed location in a last ditch attempt to find Emilie. They are followed by a now dangerously tense Zeuthen who is desperate for the operation to work and bring his daughter to safety. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

Mrs Juniper’s new dessert ‘Grasshopper Pie’ is creating quite a stir in the Big Hub. Moira has never heard of it, and after a series of misunderstandings, begins to worry that there are real grasshoppers in it! Suki is helping Daisy at the vets when there is an emergency with Mrs Anderson’s dog, Bonnie. Suki reassures and comforts the worried Mrs Anderson. Back at the Hub, a revolted Moira has decided to enforce a new rule: ‘No Grasshopper Pie in the Big Hub!’ After scaring away Mrs Juniper’s customers, Mack and Bob reassure Moira that there are no real grasshoppers in the pie, and she realises her mistake. Bonnie’s operation goes well and they discover the problem – a swallowed piece of old slipper. Daisy praises Suki for supporting Mrs Anderson, telling her that she’ll make a great vet one day. Back at the Hub, Moira has some making up

programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Aamir Tai, Miranda Sarfo Peprah, Eden Gough, William Vanderpuye, Shaheen Khan, Sahana Rameswaran-Kangesan. Preschool drama series about children living on an extraordinary inner-city estate. David is super-excited at the prospect of writing a poem for the Apple Tree community poetry event. As David tries to write about his trains, he also tries to convince his friend Laila to take part too, but no matter how hard he tries, Laila remains determined not to participate. Eventually David, with Grandma Zainab's help, works out that Laila's problem isn't stage fright but more to do with her difficulties with reading. However, David is able to rely on Mali and their friends to find a solution that allows Laila to participate.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Sid the Snail is looking for a friend to have lunch with. He’s searched everywhere, but Jago the Robin is too quick for Sid to keep up with, and the statue in the Fountain Pond isn’t one for talking. Hector the Horse just wants to play games. Will Sid ever find anyone he can eat with?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 14-month-old Arfon and Teighan as they discover the world around them and take their first fascinating steps. Arfon gets on his shoes and goes for a stroll with mum and dad to see the cows on their farm. A new pair of shoes arrives for Teighan and she decides to try them out at the local farm. Arfon’s family do an obstacle course in the garden to help him practise walking. Teighan, mum, and her sister play a barefoot game outside, and Arfon does footprints in clay with mum and dad. Finally, Arfon helps mum do a bit of gardening and Teighan finishes her day on a musical play mat. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Theo, River, Arthur, Reece, Luna and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Giovanna has got a blue bib and blue sunglasses inside her bag. All the parents and babies have different blue toys and objects in their bags too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are encouraged to hide a safe, baby-appropriate blue object or toy inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, and then play with the items together! The traditional nursery rhyme Little Boy Blue features, as does a story about Baby Bear and Baby Tiger dressing up in blue things, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring the colour blue too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory scarves and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: The Little Big Man and Clouds and Waves both by Rabindranath Tagore. Look at what my hands can do! Some tiny shoes and a silken shirt take Donna, Simon and the Magic Hands creature crew on an adventure back in time. Simon goes to Bengal where he meets a little boy who dearly wants to be a grown up, while Donna meets a little girl and her Mummy, played by Bobbles the Bear, who live by the sea and imagine playing with magical people who live in the clouds and the waves

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.desc: With a budget of £450,000, a married couple looking to uproot from Greater Manchester seek the help of Jules Hudson to find a new family home close to the coast in Dorset. Jules is in his element when he meets one of the county's blacksmiths, who specialises in restoring relics of bygone eras using traditional techniques

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team head to Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie Balmer gambles on some furniture, while Charlie Ross is excited to find a rare antique he has never seen before. Who will make the most profit at auction in Sidmouth?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the hidden history of the nurses, doctors and health workers who, for more than 70 years, have been arriving from overseas to serve in the NHS. Without them the NHS would have been in danger of collapse - not least during the current Covid-19 crisis. From its origins, the story of this beloved institution has been intertwined with one of the most divisive social and political issue of the age - immigration - and those who came to work in the NHS have found themselves fighting battles of hostility they neither sought nor expected. However, as Olusoga reveals, their stories say as much about perseverance, dedication and the overcoming of obstacles as they do about discrimination and prejudice

programme.title: Secrets of an Isis Smartphone
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar gains access to the contents of a smartphone used by three British men who left their homes to go to Syria and fight with Isis. Tthe reporter tries to understand their motivations, how they became radicalised and ultimately who is responsible for their one-way journey to certain death. Using never-before-seen videos, photos and screenshots from the device, Mobeen explores what made the trio join an estimated 900 Britons who went out to Syria and Iraq to fight with the world's most feared terrorist group

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine whether a young mother accused of poisoning her family in 1850 was hanged as a result of hearsay and gossip. Sarah Chesham had gained a reputation of a serial poisoner three years earlier when she was accused of killing three local boys using poison. She was acquitted on all charges, but the shadow of these grisly events hung over her. Ordered to stand trial for the attempted murder of her husband Richard - despite little evidence against her - Sarah, with no lawyer acting in her defence, was swiftly found guilty

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina's life is placed in danger when an attempt to lure the killer into a trap takes a deadly turn, while Jack makes a horrifying discovery about the criminal that could prove vital to his colleague's survival. Crime drama, starring Jennie Jacques and Kieran Bew

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers remember some of their favourite exotic garden rescues, featuring spaces that invited influence from far-flung countries all over the world. They include a Zambian-inspired makeover for a couple in Watford, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Newcastle who love their Lake District holidays so much they want to make the area their permanent home. With a budget of £750,000, they are looking for a property with fabulous views and enough space for visiting family. Alistair also takes to the water when he meets a passionate boat builder who restored what is thought to be the oldest passenger ferry in the world

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to Somerset to work with Vicki and Bryan, who dream of spending less time at work and more time with their young family by turning a corner of their field into a unique B&B. They have a space-age geodome, but have no idea how to transform it into a welcoming rental. Simon and his team are full of ideas, including a large wooden statement bed and set of bunks, plus a botanical theme to bring warmth and colour to the large space. It's a big and physically challenging job, but with help from friends and family the team hopes to pull off an extraordinary transformation

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy get a family pet in an attempt to teach the children to be more responsible. But as ever, things do not quite work out as planned. Domestic comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance from former EastEnders actress Annette Badland (Aunt Babe)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Billy sets up his stall, Rocky passes by and challenges him to a head-to-head battle to see who can sell the most in 10 minutes. Gray's boss Laura makes it clear she is unhappy with his performance - so he takes matters into his own hands. Suki is angry to find Honey running the Minute Mart in her absence

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A hen party on the island ends in tragedy when the bride-to-be is found dead in her bath the following morning. It doesn't take long for Humphrey to work out she was murdered - and when the electronic key proves she was the last person to open the door, it looks like she knew her killer. But why was the body in the bath? Meanwhile, Camille has a big decision to make. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring Amy Nuttall, William Ash and Ali Bastian

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Meera Sodha plus special guest Miles Jupp. Helen McGinn joins via video link and is picking the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a range of recipes to tempt family and friends, featuring classics, simple British dishes and modern favourites. In the first edition, Mary indulges her love of comfort food with heartwarming recipes including eggs Benedict Florentine, slow-cooked lamb shanks, and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots, before enjoying open-fire cooking with Swedish chef Niklas Esktedt

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent, and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Glamorgan are all sold at auction

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz's trip leads them to Naples, the spiritual home of pizza. It's a matter of days before the competition, so they need to focus on tracking down the best ingredients for their pizza. They're also keen to pick up any last minute tips, so they arrange a series of masterclasses with some of the most renowned pizzaiolos in the city

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer unravels the magic behind the combination of sugar and spice to reveal why this exotic marriage works so well in the kitchen

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Castleford Tigers (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. The Saints have defeated holders Leeds Rhinos, Huddersfield Giants and Hull FC to reach this stage and will, perhaps surprisingly given their recent success in the Super League, be looking to lift this trophy for the first since 2008. The Tigers have overcome Hull Kingston Rovers, Salford Red Devils and Warrington Wolves so far and are now aiming to end a 35-year wait to win the cup

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees a married couple from Worcester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win the money they need to get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha must decide where her heart lies, and a patient awaiting a kidney transplant from a friend deliberately sabotages his chances of a successful operation. When Ollie falls ill at school, David is plunged into a paranoid spiral as he suspects Rosa is still trying to harm Ollie - but when the boy falls ill at school, he begins to consider other possibilities

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German Army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather
