programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass revisit the case of bookmaker John Dickman, who, in 1910, became the last man to be hanged at Newcastle Prison. Bookman was found guilty of the murder of John Nisbet, who was robbed and shot five times

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Dr Sam Ma has a chest pain mystery to solve when a ship's electrician is flown to Shetland for emergency treatment - but is it a heart condition or something else? Dr Lalla mentors Dr Sam Potter as she diagnoses a Lerwick man's skin complaints

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Jack gets nearer to finding the other missing boy, and the hunt for Eddie's attacker compromises Pratt's relationship with his son. Meanwhile, there is another rape, but this time the consequences are deadly, and Gina and Jack become closer following some trouble at home

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of £450,000, a married couple looking to uproot from Greater Manchester seek the help of Jules Hudson to find a new family home close to the coast in Dorset. Jules is in his element when he meets one of the county's blacksmiths, who specialises in restoring relics of bygone eras using traditional techniques

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team head to Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie Balmer gambles on some furniture, while Charlie Ross is excited to find a rare antique he has never seen before. Who will make the most profit at auction in Sidmouth?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea and Gray cause havoc at his work function when they make the most of the free drinks, embarrassing themselves in front of Gray's boss Laura. Honey reaches out to a grief-stricken Suki, Sheree gives Lola a word of warning and Jay offers Billy an olive branch

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Parents Steph and Matt Hulme are stuck in a food rut due to their son William's serious food allergies. Following a past anaphylactic reaction, the seven-year-old cannot eat certain foods and as a result, the couple are scared to try new ingredients. Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin outline the main allergens to be aware of and suggest meals that side-step them, offering some much-needed 'kitchen confidence'. Chris also takes a visit to Hastings to find out the benefits of eating herring

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the hidden history of the nurses, doctors and health workers who, for more than 70 years, have been arriving from overseas to serve in the NHS. Without them the NHS would have been in danger of collapse - not least during the current Covid-19 crisis. From its origins, the story of this beloved institution has been intertwined with one of the most divisive social and political issue of the age - immigration - and those who came to work in the NHS have found themselves fighting battles of hostility they neither sought nor expected. However, as Olusoga reveals, their stories say as much about perseverance, dedication and the overcoming of obstacles as they do about discrimination and prejudice

programme.title: Secrets of an Isis Smartphone
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar gains access to the contents of a smartphone used by three British men who left their homes to go to Syria and fight with Isis. Tthe reporter tries to understand their motivations, how they became radicalised and ultimately who is responsible for their one-way journey to certain death. Using never-before-seen videos, photos and screenshots from the device, Mobeen explores what made the trio join an estimated 900 Britons who went out to Syria and Iraq to fight with the world's most feared terrorist group

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: The plight of asylum seekers in Scotland has been described as a humanitarian crisis. Sam Poling meets men and women who fled here to escape danger, only to find themselves facing a bleak and uncertain future. She travels to the illegal migrant camps in France to speak to those prepared to risk their lives to get to the UK, and asks whether the current system for those seeking refuge is fit for purpose

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine whether a young mother accused of poisoning her family in 1850 was hanged as a result of hearsay and gossip. Sarah Chesham had gained a reputation of a serial poisoner three years earlier when she was accused of killing three local boys using poison. She was acquitted on all charges, but the shadow of these grisly events hung over her. Ordered to stand trial for the attempted murder of her husband Richard - despite little evidence against her - Sarah, with no lawyer acting in her defence, was swiftly found guilty

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks at some of the most interesting animal-related items bought by past contestants and also heads to Peterborough to discover an extraordinary collection of horseshoes

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina's life is placed in danger when an attempt to lure the killer into a trap takes a deadly turn, while Jack makes a horrifying discovery about the criminal that could prove vital to his colleague's survival. Crime drama, starring Jennie Jacques and Kieran Bew

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers remember some of their favourite exotic garden rescues, featuring spaces that invited influence from far-flung countries all over the world. They include a Zambian-inspired makeover for a couple in Watford, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Newcastle who love their Lake District holidays so much they want to make the area their permanent home. With a budget of £750,000, they are looking for a property with fabulous views and enough space for visiting family. Alistair also takes to the water when he meets a passionate boat builder who restored what is thought to be the oldest passenger ferry in the world

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team head to Somerset to work with Vicki and Bryan, who dream of spending less time at work and more time with their young family by turning a corner of their field into a unique B&B. They have a space-age geodome, but have no idea how to transform it into a welcoming rental. Simon and his team are full of ideas, including a large wooden statement bed and set of bunks, plus a botanical theme to bring warmth and colour to the large space. It's a big and physically challenging job, but with help from friends and family the team hopes to pull off an extraordinary transformation

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy get a family pet in an attempt to teach the children to be more responsible. But as ever, things do not quite work out as planned. Domestic comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with a guest appearance from former EastEnders actress Annette Badland (Aunt Babe)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Billy sets up his stall, Rocky passes by and challenges him to a head-to-head battle to see who can sell the most in 10 minutes. Gray's boss Laura makes it clear she is unhappy with his performance - so he takes matters into his own hands. Suki is angry to find Honey running the Minute Mart in her absence

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A hen party on the island ends in tragedy when the bride-to-be is found dead in her bath the following morning. It doesn't take long for Humphrey to work out she was murdered - and when the electronic key proves she was the last person to open the door, it looks like she knew her killer. But why was the body in the bath? Meanwhile, Camille has a big decision to make. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring Amy Nuttall, William Ash and Ali Bastian

programme.title: A Simple Favour (2018)
programme.desc: Stephanie Smothers, a widowed single mother who runs a crafts and recipes vlog, becomes friends with Emily Nelson, a public relations director for a fashion company. Emily has a work crisis and asks Stephanie to babysit her son, but later goes missing amid suggestions from colleagues she has jetted off to Miami. Feeling perplexed, Stephanie attempts to get to the bottom of her friend's mysterious actions. Paul Feig's comedy thriller based on Darcey Bell's novel, starring Anna Kendrick, Blake Lively and Henry Golding

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Meera Sodha plus special guest Miles Jupp. Helen McGinn joins via video link and is picking the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a range of recipes to tempt family and friends, featuring classics, simple British dishes and modern favourites. In the first edition, Mary indulges her love of comfort food with heartwarming recipes including eggs Benedict Florentine, slow-cooked lamb shanks, and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots, before enjoying open-fire cooking with Swedish chef Niklas Esktedt

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent, and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Glamorgan are all sold at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Richard Madley helps two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: St Helens v Castleford Tigers (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. The Saints have defeated holders Leeds Rhinos, Huddersfield Giants and Hull FC to reach this stage and will, perhaps surprisingly given their recent success in the Super League, be looking to lift this trophy for the first since 2008. The Tigers have overcome Hull Kingston Rovers, Salford Red Devils and Warrington Wolves so far and are now aiming to end a 35-year wait to win the cup

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees a married couple from Worcester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win the money they need to get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha must decide where her heart lies, and a patient awaiting a kidney transplant from a friend deliberately sabotages his chances of a successful operation. When Ollie falls ill at school, David is plunged into a paranoid spiral as he suspects Rosa is still trying to harm Ollie - but when the boy falls ill at school, he begins to consider other possibilities

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German Army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German Army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolan's wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.desc: An appreciation of the work of Dr John MacInnes. Formerly based in the School of Scottish Studies in Edinburgh, he has collected the oral literature and tradition of Gaelic speakers throughout Scotland

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod investigates the landscape, language and culture of Dundonnell and Fisherfield Forest, a woodland lacking in permanent settlements which covers a large mountainous area of Wester Ross and is principally used for deer stalking

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Donald Macsween is back with a special series from his croft in Ness. Cameras follow him from the beginning of the year and into lockdown, getting his thoughts on how the coronavirus has impacted him in his job as a full-time crofter

programme.title: Folk Prom
programme.desc: Part one. In a Prom that celebrates the history and evolution of the folk music scene in Britain and Ireland, the BBC Concert Orchestra collaborates with Julie Fowlis, the Unthanks, Sam Lee and Jarlath Henderson who are pushing the boundaries of folk music

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: The journey undertaken by three sisters - Julia, Yulissa and Kenya - who live on the east coast of Nicaragua and climb into their dug-out boat to row to school every morning

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum takes a walk on the Moray Coast to explore some caves, hoping to capture a photo of some Pictish carvings in Sculptor's Cave

programme.title: Ceòl Dùthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts set up shop in Llangollen, Denbighshire, where Jan Keyne uses the power of ice cream to sway a deal, Alison Chapman gets served up some silver - and her name in print - and Fay Rutter some novelty pipes

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends from a London gospel choir answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four more contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Kevin, Jay, Laura and Daniel must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The jury reaches its verdict in Jimmy's trial. Mandy fights her feelings, and Meena is thrilled

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: As budgets and waistbands get tighter, each episode features a new family who take on the Eat, Shop, Save eight week challenge; to eat better, get fitter and save money. This week, presenter Ranvir Singh and her team of experts are in Derbyshire to help a family whose son and pet dog are eating more vegetables than the parents

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jimmy and Nicola may have reached the end of the road. Nicola's anger boils over, and April experiences a backlash online

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.desc: Martin Clunes learns about animal exploitation - the good, the bad and the ugly. In Sri Lanka, fishing manta rays is legal but a turtle sanctuary offers hope. In Thailand, he learns more about silk production, and a more animal-friendly alternative, and on the Australian island of Friday Island, he is shown the delicate art of growing pearls. Finally, in the Abrolhos islands, Martin learns about the most valuable catch in Australia's waters

programme.title: Diana's Decades
programme.desc: Princess Diana began the 1980s a shy bride and ended the decade the most photographed woman in the world. From Dynasty Di to Pop Princess to Aids champion, Diana harnessed and embodied the spirit of the 80s. As the world screamed 'Greed is Good', Diana found her voice and championed those left behind, and became, in the words of Vanity Fair's Tina Brown, 'the mouse that roared'

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Late Debate
programme.desc: Simon Harris is joined by a panel of local MPs for a discussion on the latest news and events at Westminster

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides an Africa Twin through the backroads of Oxfordshire, road-tests his first ever electric motorbike, and reveals Alan Milyard's extraordinary new Kawasaki

programme.desc: Action from stage 18 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a mountainous 129.7km route from Pau to Luz Ardiden. programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Shelagh, Holly, Daniel and Norman pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jimmy struggles with admitting the truth. Meena is fuming, and Tracy pours out her feelings to Nate

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police question Paul about the stolen heat pump. Having kept £1,000 from the heat pump money for himself, Todd tells Billy and Summer he got a bonus from work and he's going to spend it on a family holiday. An angry Alina confronts Fiz, pointing out that she never set out to steal Tyrone from her. Curtis realises he's miscounted and signed up 13 models for the calendar

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are Madonna, Dave Bautista, Mel Giedroyc, Peter Kay, the Spice Girls, Harrison Ford, Glenn Close, Mo Gilligan, Keira Knightley and Bear Grylls

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Host Jeremy Clarkson may also lend a hand, if the players trust his general knowledge

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 19 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 207km route from Mourenx to Libourne. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge, Nina Wadia and Dominic Monaghan join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: It's the biggest sporting event in Grimsby - the bog roll roll. All Dave has to do is chase the bog roll down the hill and catch it.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds don't care about a carbon footprint, since George has come home with a haul of free electronics that they all want to test out.

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: LEGO Hidden Side
programme.desc: Our heroes investigate a carnival after hours, but the ghost finds Jack and Parker first, knocking them out in a spooky hall of mirrors.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike rescues Fluffy from the raccoon's lair but he doesn't notice that the kitty has pocketed one the racoon's favourite toys. Fluffy and the turtles are in love with their new musical toy! The indignant raccoons turn up at the Mikkelsens looking for a way to get it back. How dare someone steal their stuff! But they didn't count on how hard Fluffy and the turtles will fight to keep their new favourite toy out of the racoon's hands.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced into action when the raccoons steal the turtles to help Freddy hold up his newly made throne. The crazy raccoon envisions himself a king and thinks the turtles are the perfect finishing touch to his scheme. Mike sneaks into the raccoons' lair to steal back his friends. With Iris's help, he intends to teach the raccoons a lesson they won't forget.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis smells like rainbows and puppy dog tails. She's got The Vapours, the next event in a vampire's afterlife. (S2 Ep3b)

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly and her daughter Rosie Smith, and Candice Brown join Martin and Roman Kemp, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Radio 1 star Mollie King and chefs Ravneet Gill and Tony Tobin. He demonstrates recipes for prawn jalfrezi, bolognese and chocolate fudge cake, while Ravneet and Tony whip up some dishes of their own

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cookery series where famous faces will be cooking up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the celebrity contestants are paired with a professional chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. In the first episode, Johnny Vegas, McFly's Harry Judd, Shirley Ballas and Naughty Boy take part

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Marsh Cup from Newbury, and races at 1.50, 3.00 and 3.40. Plus, three races from Market Rasen and the 3.25 Irish Oaks from The Curragh. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Mick Fitzgerald, reporting from Matt Chapman, Sally Ann Grassick and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles and Mark Johnson

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A man is hit by a falling sofa, a girl squirts glue into her eyes and a toddler fires a rocket into his sister's face

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Tom Allen, Melvin Odoom and Patti Clare answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Giles Coren, Rachel Johnson, Chris Packham and Josie Long pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show in which three members of the public play a giant arcade machine where everything can change on the roll of a coin, with Martine McCutcheon, Jason Manford and AJ Adudu helping the contestants

programme.title: The Void
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo and Fleur East host the game show in which contestants take on a wide range of demanding mental and physical challenges to win a jackpot of £25,000 - all while navigating the terrifying prospect of falling into a huge tank holding 520,000 litres of water called the Void

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat, with four lifelines and just 15 questions standing between them and a million pounds

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson takes a look at the highs and lows of the small screen, judging shouty Americans, manufactured jeopardy and Tony Robinson. Back in the studio, he wins a staring match with a dog and is very brave when he is stung on the backside by an errant bee.

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Bridget's focus on single life and her career is interrupted when she finds herself pregnant, but with one hitch - she can only be 50 per cent sure of the identity of her baby's father after a pair of one-night stands. Is the dad old flame Mark Darcy or US dating guru Jack Qwant? Comedy sequel, starring Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey, Emma Thompson and Sally Phillips

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 20th and penultimate stage of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 30.8km individual time-trial from Libourne to Saint-Emilion. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through the quarter-finals, two families go head to head in the second semi-final of the tournament, hosted by Anne Hegerty, aiming to move a step closer to winning the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family and a holiday of a lifetime. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

The crazy raccoon envisions himself a king and thinks the turtles are the perfect finishing touch to his scheme. Mike sneaks into the raccoons' lair to steal back his friends. With Iris's help, he intends to teach the raccoons a lesson they won't forget.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis smells like rainbows and puppy dog tails. She's got The Vapours, the next event in a vampire's afterlife. (S2 Ep3b)

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly and her daughter Rosie Smith, and Candice Brown join Martin and Roman Kemp, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Radio 1 star Mollie King and chefs Ravneet Gill and Tony Tobin. He demonstrates recipes for prawn jalfrezi, bolognese and chocolate fudge cake, while Ravneet and Tony whip up some dishes of their own

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cookery series where famous faces will be cooking up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the celebrity contestants are paired with a professional chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. In the first episode, Johnny Vegas, McFly's Harry Judd, Shirley Ballas and Naughty Boy take part

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by friend and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, riding around Jersey on mini motorbikes while exploring the island's culinary delights. James samples one of Jersey's most famous exports - the Jersey Royal potato - which he uses to cook up a squid risotto with a difference

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Marsh Cup from Newbury, and races at 1.50, 3.00 and 3.40. Plus, three races from Market Rasen and the 3.25 Irish Oaks from The Curragh. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Mick Fitzgerald, reporting from Matt Chapman, Sally Ann Grassick and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles and Mark Johnson

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A man is hit by a falling sofa, a girl squirts glue into her eyes and a toddler fires a rocket into his sister's face

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard asks the questions as Tom Allen, Melvin Odoom and Patti Clare answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Giles Coren, Rachel Johnson, Chris Packham and Josie Long pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show in which three members of the public play a giant arcade machine where everything can change on the roll of a coin, with Martine McCutcheon, Jason Manford and AJ Adudu helping the contestants

programme.title: The Void
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo and Fleur East host the game show in which contestants take on a wide range of demanding mental and physical challenges to win a jackpot of £25,000 - all while navigating the terrifying prospect of falling into a huge tank holding 520,000 litres of water called the Void

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat, with four lifelines and just 15 questions standing between them and a million pounds

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson takes a look at the highs and lows of the small screen, judging shouty Americans, manufactured jeopardy and Tony Robinson. Back in the studio, he wins a staring match with a dog and is very brave when he is stung on the backside by an errant bee.

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Bridget's focus on single life and her career is interrupted when she finds herself pregnant, but with one hitch - she can only be 50 per cent sure of the identity of her baby's father after a pair of one-night stands. Is the dad old flame Mark Darcy or US dating guru Jack Qwant? Comedy sequel, starring Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey, Emma Thompson and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 20th and penultimate stage of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 30.8km individual time-trial from Libourne to Saint-Emilion. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Filmed over two years, this documentary follows the Manchester soap's transfer from its 53-year-old base at Granada Studios to its new home in Trafford Park. Featuring behind-the-scenes access as stars of the show pack up their dressing rooms and film their final scenes at the old site. The programme also includes rarely seen footage of Coronation Street being shot in the 1960s, and tells the story of the evolution of filming the long-running series. Featuring contributions by Beverley Callard, Sally Dynevor, Simon Gregson and Brooke Vincent

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Filmed over two years, this documentary follows the Manchester soap's transfer from its 53-year-old base at Granada Studios to its new home in Trafford Park. Featuring behind-the-scenes access as stars of the show pack up their dressing rooms and film their final scenes at the old site. The programme also includes rarely seen footage of Coronation Street being shot in the 1960s, and tells the story of the evolution of filming the long-running series. Featuring contributions by Beverley Callard, Sally Dynevor, Simon Gregson and Brooke Vincent

programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: There is excitement throughout the house and nearby village as the annual cricket match draws near, and the event brings out Robert's competitive side. Violet's great-niece Rose arrives, but a trip to London reveals there is more to her than meets the eye, while Matthew and Mary seem to be keeping secrets from each other. Thomas's future appears to be uncertain, but he may have found an unexpected ally in Bates

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: In the Christmas special from 2012, Robert and his family make their annual summer visit to his Scottish cousins at Duneagle Castle, leaving most of their servants at home. Meanwhile, at Downton, Carson faces the task of keeping the staff focused on their work polishing the silverware and cleaning, but with the Granthams hundreds of miles away, minds begin to wander to other things, including the forthcoming Thirsk Country Fair

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. When the detective suffers a personal loss, he's forced to navigate a complex murder case while grieving. A young Estonian woman has been found dead, with the evidence suggesting she was buried alive. Piecing together her movements, the team uncovers a dark world of prostitution and drugs, and investigates her connection to a single father, his autistic son, and a family who seem to have silenced the town

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers are dismayed to learn the arson attack on the Osgoods' house has injured Michael and killed his son Robbie, and local bully Gary McCready is soon questioned. As the investigation into Katrin's murder continues, the body of an older man who died of natural causes is found buried near her grave, and when Banks suspects Jason McCready abducted missing Annika, he uncovers a shocking secret. Meanwhile, the detective gives a moving speech at his mother's funeral, and the ceremony brings him closer to his father

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.desc: Extended edition of the soap

programme.title: Bless This House (1972)
programme.desc: Middle-aged father Sid Abbott's dream of building an illegal whisky still in the shed is continually frustrated by the antics of his son and daughter. However, he has a bigger problem in store when new neighbours move in next door and conflict instantly erupts. Comedy, based on the sitcom, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Sally Geeson

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Called to investigate the attempted theft of a headless skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe finds himself forced to deal with landowners whose archaic values are jarringly at odds with the modern world. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'the Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The detective interrogates a mysterious Dane who has been caught with the dead body of a diamond dealer in his car, and who proves entirely resistant to his questioning. Maigret is convinced his suspect's sister may know something, but soon discovers that everyone in the small rural town where they live is hiding something

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry and Louisa return to England. While his mother is reunited with her cousins, Larry returns to London, where he falls in with a group of Bohemian artists - including writer Henry Miller. Back in Corfu, Leslie gets offered a job as a policeman, Gerry saves a donkey from an abusive farmer, and Spiro forbids Margo from seeing Zoltan

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's cousin visits the Abbott household, and the bemused family have a terrible time getting rid of him. Sid James stars

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly is finding it difficult to face up to her problems and can see only one way of solving them. Zoe and Ashley compare notes on their relationship progress so far

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin could be about to lose a girlfriend and gain a sister-in-law. Last-ever episode of the comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick, Jo and Katie prepare to leave Aidensfield, while mysterious bumps in the night make Greengrass wonder if his house is haunted. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers discuss Kelly's predicament, each blaming themselves for letting it get this far. Ollie persuades Angie to let her go to the cinema with her new boyfriend

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy tells Mandy she is the only one who can help Kelly. Betty asks Reggie to stop interfering in her relationship with Seth. Andy is upset to discover that Ollie has a date

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley is upset that everyone has reacted so hostile towards her, and Linda refuses to let Hayley use the ladies' toilet. Les tries to stop Toyah working for Roy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Hayley feels she cannot cope and tells Roy she is leaving Weatherfield. Liz admits to Deirdre she does not love Jim anymore. Des and Les get drunk at the stag party

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin could be about to lose a girlfriend and gain a sister-in-law. Last-ever episode of the comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: There is excitement throughout the house and nearby village as the annual cricket match draws near, and the event brings out Robert's competitive side. Violet's great-niece Rose arrives, but a trip to London reveals there is more to her than meets the eye, while Matthew and Mary seem to be keeping secrets from each other. Thomas's future appears to be uncertain, but he may have found an unexpected ally in Bates

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the murder of a prostitute, but their only clues are a pornographic film and a Eastern bloc-backed plot to blackmail a prominent politician tipped to become a future prime minister. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 17 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a hilly 178.4km route from Muret to Saint-Lary-Soulan. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage 18 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a mountainous 129.7km route from Pau to Luz Ardiden. programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood presents action from obscure motorsports, such as bus and caravan racing and ice speedway

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Elton Welsby presents extended highlights of the memorable match at Anfield, which saw Arsenal needing to win by at least two goals to pip their opponents to the title. With analysis from Bobby Robson, reports by Jim Rosenthal and commentary from Brian Moore and David Pleat

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole goes riding in the Yorkshire Dales and tells the story of American motorcycle brand Indian. He also begins the rebuild of his vintage BSA Goldstar

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A former agent regresses to a time when he was captured and brain-washed by the KGB into killing four of his colleagues. programme.desc: CI5 investigates a judge's death and finds a connection with a deadly terrorist group of young middle-class drop-outs that has declared war on the legal system. programme.title: The Professionals

Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Bourne Supremacy (2004)

programme.title: The Bourne Supremacy (2004)
programme.desc: The amnesiac assassin is living quietly in hiding, until his former employers come after him again and murder his girlfriend. Action thriller sequel, starring Matt Damon, Brian Cox, Joan Allen and Julia Stiles

Action adventure sequel, starring Bruce Willis and Jai Courtney

In this first programme, boxing champion Chris Eubank is tasked with memorising hundreds of facts about Jurassic dinosaurs, Strictly Come Dancing legend Len Goodman is given '90s American hip-hop to master, Love Island winner Amber Gill is assigned British birds, Naked Attraction presenter Anna Richardson is tasked with the solar system, while actress Nina Wadia must memorise the human skeleton. Hosted by Sandi Toksvig

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Amy bring sunshine to her summer house by transforming if from a tired, dull space into a tropical room filled with a kaleidoscope of colour, complete with original artwork projected onto the walls and cactus-inspired cushions. Dick also responds to a request from Sophia and Jenny to help build a bird hide and periscopes, so the children at their forest school can go birdwatching. Back at the chateau, Angel creates a mermaid-inspired play shed for a youngster's birthday

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay: Uncharted
programme.desc: The celebrated TV chef learns about the secrets of ancient Berber cuisine in Morocco's Middle Atlas Mountains, where he dives into the food culture and ancient traditions of the indigenous Amazigh - Berber - people. Gordon rappels over a waterfall to meet mushroom hunters and milks a camel

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Police officers in Northamptonshire respond to an ever increasing number of 999 calls concerning people living in shared accommodation. The number of houses of multiple occupancy (HMO) has nearly trebled since 2013. PC Sean Whelan is dispatched to one HMO to deal with a fight in the shared kitchen. He also has to mediate between a householder who hasn't been paid the rent he's owed and a tenant with no contract in place. PC Debbie Wishart attends a hostel where she arrests a woman suspected of stealing a chicken Kiev, while PC Sam Clulow attends a call involving a vulnerable heroin user who's being preyed upon

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. The concluding episode examines how DNA found on Joanne Lees' shirt and the couple's Kombi van were decisive in the murder trial of Bradley Murdoch, but even with this evidence, there are serious questions still to be answered in this controversial case

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family-run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of contestants from Brighton compete, beginning with Sam and Nick, who produce a simple menu that reflects their busy lives running two businesses and looking after four children. The second couple are Maria and Terry, who offer an Asian-themed meal and an evening's entertainment of writing love poems. Last to host are Dave and his perfectionist girlfriend Sarah, who try to provide a fun-fuelled night full of quality ingredients

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A sideshow circus wife, whose dangerous occupation demands precision, swaps lives with a frazzled mother of four whose own home life is in pandemonium

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team investigates issues surrounding fast food. Matt Tebbutt discovers why takeaway chips tend to go soggy, and Kate Quilton examines the challenges of ensuring every branch of a pizza chain produces exactly the same food. The programme also investigates reports of parasites in sushi and discovers why burgers cannot be served rare in the same way that a steak can

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk discovers how the Victorians instituted a revolution in law and order, created the first modern police force and built the prisons Britain still relies on today. He begins in Spike Island, Co Cork, dubbed 'Ireland's Alcatraz', where he learns about the terrible conditions endured by convicts awaiting transportation to Australia, and hears the stories of those who tried to escape. Next, Michael travels to Crumlin Road Gaol in Belfast, discovering why the Victorians embarked on the biggest prison-building programme in British history - and why their efforts at prisoner rehabilitation didn't always succeed. And in Glasgow, he discovers why the city became home to the country's first recognisably modern police force

programme.title: Today at the Great Yorkshire Show
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Jules Hudson meet fourth-generation farmer Stephen Short, who has won countless prizes for his animals over the years. In 2018, his beloved sheep won the coveted prizes of Hampshire Down Male and Female Champion, but after a less successful outing in 2019, will he be able to return to his former glory this year and make his ancestors proud? Cameras also follow Emily Turner and her four-year-old son Jack as they compete for the first time with their Golden Guernsey goats Stacey and Clementine

programme.title: Lie with Me

programme.desc: Ben travels to New Zealand's North Island, where he meets a young nomadic family led by Amber and Andy, who spend their life exploring the country's wild landscapes. He hears how the family's lives changed forever after one fateful day, how they swapped the stress of the city for a life on the open road, the price they paid for chasing their dream and how the horrors of the past still haunt them today

programme.title: Never Teach Your Wife to Drive
programme.desc: Three men set out to teach their partners the rules of the road, while state-of-the-art in-car cameras record events including blazing arguments and near misses, and the drama and mayhem of the lessons puts added strain on their relationships. At 32, Kat Fleming-Scott is a late starter at driving, but her husband Chris's map-reading leaves a lot to be desired when he gets them hopelessly lost in Liverpool, while Weymouth mother-of-four Lauren Foster has lessons from her spouse Adam, who believes men are much the better drivers. Meanwhile, Nathan Phillips is a worrier and when partner Laura Whitfield becomes overconfident behind the wheel, he begins to show signs of regretting his decision to teach her

programme.title: Nick's Quest

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: The adventurer meets more people who have turned their backs on the rat-race and set up home in some of the most remote locations on Earth. This week, Ben travels to the vast forests of the Rhodope Mountains in Bulgaria to join Miriam and Peter, a couple who have chosen life as nomads, hiking through some of the world's most striking wildernesses. They are educated but unemployed and homeless - and it's just the way they like it. Having previously walked the length of New Zealand in 2016, Ben now finds the duo on an intrepid journey across South Eastern Europe

programme.title: Scotland: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: It is late January and in the western Highlands of Scotland, and it's unusually warm, even at night. The warmth has brought red deer out of hiding. With no snow or bitter winds to contend with, they're heading back to the hilltops, where there's plenty of heather to eat. Cameras also focus on the Shetland Isles during the winter solstice, and mountain hares in the Cairngorms

programme.title: Wonderful Wales with Michael Ball
programme.desc: The singer and broadcaster heads to mid Wales, making his way to the north of the country. His first stop is a familiar seaside town he is extremely fond of - Aberystwyth. This is where he did his first paid acting job, and Michael meets a friendly face from his summer performing in Godspell. Next, he drops in for lunch with the spectacular red kites of Mid Wales. Michael then checks in to restaurant Ynyshir on the Dyfi estuary for a Michelin-starred experience hosted by multi-award-winning chef Gareth Ward

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: Using the latest sonar technology, an expedition to map sunken Allied vessels off the Normandy coast reveals stunning images from beneath the waves. There are more than 100 shipwrecks in the English Channel of the landing craft and ships that took part in the greatest amphibious invasion fleet ever assembled

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: A celebration of the most successful singles of 1998, with contributions from Peter Andre, Steps' Lee Latchford-Evans and Hannah Spearritt from S Club 7. The Spice Girls waved Goodbye, Fat Les had a hit with Vindaloo, and Robbie Williams stormed the charts with Angels and Millennium, but who had the biggest-selling single of the year?

programme.title: The Story of the Songs
programme.desc: The stories behind three singles of the singles that made the singer a global superstar. The programme examines 1996 breakthrough ballad Because You Loved Me, operatic rock track It's All Coming Back to Me Now with its accompanying Gothic music video, and her 2003 cover of I Drove All Night, which drew inspiration from Swedish dance music

programme.desc: Deep into the jungle mountains of Thailand on the border with Myanmar and far off any conventional map, Ben spends time with 40-year-old French-Canadian Julien, who has been a Buddhist monk for almost half his life. Living in a remote monastery, Ben learns how and why this former teenage student from the West stripped away all complications of a modern life for a primitive Eastern and religious existence, and how this choice affected his family. The adventurer observes how Julien has created a bespoke way of life, which includes giving back to the remote hill-tribe communities in his region

Among the highlights are artist blacksmith Katie Ventress undertaking her biggest challenge yet, and The Yorkshire Vet star Peter Wright commentating on the sheep in the show ring

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild UK
programme.desc: Ben meets 40-year-old Amanda Owen from Huddersfield, who left behind a comfortable existence to pursue her lifelong dream of becoming a hill shepherdess. Despite having no background in farming, she now lives at a 2,000-acre farm in the Yorkshire Dales with husband Clive and their eight children. Ben is given a taste of life as a hill shepherd, learns the highs and lows of farming life and finds out how she copes looking after such a huge family as well as her 1,000 native Swaledale sheep

programme.title: William & Harry: Princes at War?

programme.title: Charles & Di's Wedding: The Revelations
programme.desc: Documentary examining the week leading up to the royal wedding of Prince Charles and Lady Diana Spencer in July 1981, which lifted the spirits of the nation during a turbulent time. Interviewees include people who knew the couple, such as Mary Robertson, Col Robert Spencer and Angela Rippon, who carried out a pre-wedding interview as well as presenting the live coverage on the day
