programme.title: Milkshake! Monkey's Amazing Adventures
programme.desc: Monkey visits Lincolnshire, where he learns all about wind and how to fly his kite

programme.title: Timberjack (1954)
programme.desc: A man forced to return to his Montana home to take over the family lumber business sets out to discover the reasons behind his father's death, while engaging in a fierce professional and romantic rivalry with a neighbouring forester. Western drama, starring Sterling Hayden, David Brian, Vera Ralston and Hoagy Carmichael

programme.title: The Furies (1950)
programme.desc: The daughter of a ruthlessly ambitious cattle baron is divided between her love for one of his enemies and the prospect of taking over the family ranch when he dies. But when the cruel rancher has one of her friends hanged, she begins plotting his downfall. Western from director Anthony Mann, starring Barbara Stanwyck and Walter Huston

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles (1959)
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson are asked to protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskerville family, who is believed to be the target of a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Hammer version of Arthur Conan Doyle's mystery, starring Peter Cushing, Andre Morrell, Christopher Lee, John Le Mesurier and Miles Malleson

programme.title: Carry On Spying (1964)
programme.desc: A secret formula is stolen by a sinister organisation masterminded by the evil Dr Crow. The British government plans to assign its top agents to deal with the crisis, but owing to staff shortages, has to settle for a bungling spy and his three hapless trainees. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Cribbins, Jim Dale, Eric Barker and Victor Maddern

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Fallout (2018)
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and the IMF team join forces with CIA assassin August Walker to prevent a disaster caused by mysterious arms dealer John Lark and a group of terrorists known as the Apostles. However, Hunt and his colleagues are soon pushed to their limits thanks to a face from their past. Writer/director Christopher McQuarrie's sixth entry in the action thriller saga, starring Tom Cruise and Henry Cavill

programme.title: Prospect (2018)
programme.desc: A teenager and her father travel to a remote alien moon aiming to strike it rich, but there are others roving the wilderness and the job quickly devolves into a fight to survive. Sci-fi thriller, starring Sophie Thatcher, Jay Duplass and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: Fire Will Come (2019)
programme.desc: A man returns home to his aging mother after serving a prison sentence for arson. Along the way, he tries to adapt to daily farm life and cope with the villagers' memories of his past actions. Crime drama, starring Amador Arias, Benedicta Sánchez and Inazio Abrao. In Galician and Spanish

programme.title: The Blue Dahlia (1946)
programme.desc: A war-weary pilot returns home from service in Europe, but his welcome is far from warm. Not only must he deal with the news of his wife's infidelities, but he also finds himself accused of her murder. However, the wife of his dead spouse's lover is on hand to help him prove his innocence. Thriller, written by Raymond Chandler and starring Alan Ladd, Veronica Lake, William Bendix and Doris Dowling

programme.title: The Duel at Silver Creek (1952)
programme.desc: A gambler goes after the gang of claim jumpers who killed his father, and joins forces with an injured marshal who also wants the criminals brought to justice. However, their partnership is tested when a devious femme fatale enters their lives. Don Siegel's Western, starring Audie Murphy, Stephen McNally and Faith Domergue

programme.title: Texas Lady (1955)
programme.desc: A woman arrives in Texas to take over the local newspaper, and vows to use it to expose corruption. Her first target is the gambler she wrongly holds responsible for her father's suicide, but who proves an unlikely ally when she faces the real villains - two cattle barons who rule the town with an iron fist. Western, with Claudette Colbert, Barry Sullivan and Gregory Walcott

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest (1960)
programme.desc: Legendary outlaw hero Robin Hood rallies his merry men to thwart a dastardly plan by the Sheriff of Nottingham to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury, who has denounced the sheriff's plot to confiscate another nobleman's land. Medieval adventure, starring Richard Greene, Peter Cushing, Niall MacGinnis, Richard Pasco, Sarah Branch and Oliver Reed

programme.title: Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen (2009)
programme.desc: The war between the shape-shifting robots erupts once again, as the first of the evil Decepticons leads an attack on Earth to locate an ancient planet-destroying machine. Hapless teenager Sam Witwicky is caught in the middle of the ensuing conflict as he holds the key to discovering the location of the device. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Shia LaBeouf and Megan Fox

programme.title: X-Men: Apocalypse (2016)
programme.desc: The mutant heroes face the oldest and most powerful of their kind, an ancient Egyptian tyrant who is restored to life in the 1980s and plans to destroy civilisation and rebuild it in his own image. Superhero adventure sequel, starring James McAvoy, Michael Fassbender, Jennifer Lawrence and Oscar Isaac

programme.title: The Purge: Election Year (2016)
programme.desc: A US senator is targeted for her anti-Purge agenda, a shop owner is forced to defend his store when his Purge Night insurance becomes unaffordable, and the US economy is boosted by tourists arriving to take part in the annual night of murder and mayhem. Action horror sequel, starring Frank Grillo, Mykelti Williamson and Elizabeth Mitchell

programme.title: Feedback (2019)
programme.desc: Jarvis Dolan, the star of successful late-night radio show The Grim Reality, experiences the worst night of his life when two armed men burst into his studio and take control of the show. He is forced to continue with the live programme, following the script of the assailants. Horror thriller, starring Eddie Marsan, Paul Anderson and Ivana Baquero

programme.title: Snoopy and Charlie Brown: The Peanuts Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The long-suffering youngster hopes his lifelong run of bad luck will come to an end when he develops a crush on a new schoolfriend. His beloved dog has more important things to worry about, as he dreams of taking to the skies in his airborne kennel. Animated comedy, with the voices of Noah Schnapp and Bill Melendez

programme.title: Carry On Sergeant (1958)
programme.desc: A military sergeant approaching retirement hopes to end his career on a high note by seeing his last lot of recruits come first in an annual competition to find the best squad in the forces. However, he is not prepared for the hopeless band of misfits placed under his command. The first in the comedy series, starring William Hartnell and Bob Monkhouse

programme.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (2013)
programme.desc: An ordinary man seeks solace from his mundane life by drifting into daydreams where he is a heroic figure. When he and a friend are faced with unemployment, he is propelled into a globetrotting adventure in real life. Comedy drama based on James Thurber's short story, directed by and starring Ben Stiller. With Kristen Wiig, Adam Scott and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: The Adventures of Tintin (2011)
programme.desc: A reporter's purchase of a model ship brings him under threat from a ruthless criminal, as it is one of a series of clues to the location of the real vessel, which holds a pirate's lost treasure. He sets off on a globetrotting quest to find it, assisted by a drunken sea captain, two bumbling detectives and his faithful dog. Steven Spielberg's animated adventure based on Herge's comic-strip series, with the voices of Jamie Bell, Andy Serkis and Daniel Craig

programme.title: Warcraft (2016)
programme.desc: A kingdom stands on the brink of war when it is invaded by a vast horde of creatures who have travelled through a portal from their dying world. As dark magic works its influence on the world, two leaders from either side are set on a collision course with each other as they strive to protect their people. Fantasy adventure based on the best-selling computer game, starring Travis Fimmel, Paula Patton and Ben Foster

programme.title: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
programme.desc: The story of Frank Abagnale Jr, who found notoriety as a teenager conning and charming his way across America. programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: Jerry becomes ill after accidentally ingesting mutant bacteria. The family rushes him to hospital in space, where he is given the option of making a huge personal sacrifice to save the life of an alien civil rights leader. Rick makes repairs to the cable TV box in the hospital waiting room, enabling visitors to watch interdimensional shows

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: The duo visit another planet to purchase wiper fluid and witness an annual event where the local populace commits crimes for one night without consequence. Jerry and Summer work on their father-daughter relationship, but his lack of employment keeps coming up

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from the first series, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. Among the shows under review are Love Island, Naked Attraction, Killing Eve and First Dates, as well as the films Titanic and American Pie

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Daydreamer Yanis wants his wedding to be a towering success as he plans to tie the knot at the Eiffel Tower. However, his hard-to-please bride Chanise has a tranquil wedding in the Scottish countryside in mind. As this controlling bride struggles to hand over the reins, in a series first, the wedding plans are leaked and the couple are left facing the consequences

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre tries to get Bow a meaningful gift for Valentine's Day, while Ruby teaches Jack how to get a girl after she hears about his love troubles

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Bow is heartbroken to learn that Diane is no longer friends with Sophie, and Dre discovers a hole in the wall that Junior and Jack were hiding

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is taught how to bind books by expert Isabelle Ting, and reveals how to create a notebook from leftover materials including leather offcuts, recycled leather coats and decorative paper

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detective team searches for an heir to the estate of a woman who led a glamorous life of international travel and adventure. There is the story of an East End couple who chose to raise their disabled daughter at a time when it was against conventional wisdom, plus details of unclaimed estates still awaiting heirs

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Surrey and Middlesex. Henry restores a pair of racing go-karts to race against restorer Guy, and Simon repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole refurbishes a classic Ford Mustang and a neon garage sign, while Simon O'Brien restores a classic Chopper bicycle and turns a bass guitar into a chair

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Eastbourne on the south coast to restore a 1962 Mini Cooper. This particular model is the property of farmer Keith, who has raced and rallied cars for most of his life, until being struck down with serious heart problems. Although his racing days are now over, Keith cannot let go of his beloved Mini, even though it has been rotting away in a shed for years

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A mountain biker has a bad fall in a remote part of a forest, a four-year-old needs strong pain relief when he breaks his thigh bone, and a farmer fractures a replacement hip

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at North Bridge Street B&B in Hawick in the Scottish Borders, which is run by Eileen Walsh and her Portuguese partner Emanuel Forte. When the guests arrive, the purple-themed rooms cause a reaction, while one competitor's constant criticising irritates another

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at the Golden Fleece in Ruleholme, Cumbria, run by chef Robert Cowan and his partner, Sylvia Spark. Before long, guests Eileen and Emmanuel pick up on cleanliness issues, and breakfast proves to be a disappointment

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Bubble Inn in Stenson, Derbyshire for the third day of the competition, where unsightly stains and security leave some of them less than impressed. Manager and ex-semi-professional boxer Connie Eleftheriou is keen to take his rivals in the ring with him, however the real damage happens when Emmanuel is taken to hospital after some overenthusiastic dancing at a Greek-themed night

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Melinda Beckett-Hughes is the final host of the week at Ayuda House in Greater Manchester - and as she has spent the week picking fault, the guests expect perfection from her. However, Robert and Sylvia are shocked to find a dirty towel in a drawer, while Emmanuel and Eileen cannot believe the levels of dust and Connie is horrified by the carpet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some old scores. Eileen and Emmanuel from North Bridge Street want to get to the bottom of Melinda and Stewart's dirty bin comments as well as challenging Melinda's shower issues. Robert and Sylvia from the Golden Fleece try to defend their breakfast disaster, but also pull Eileen and Emmanuel up on their low score

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Aaron and Christine Green create an unconventional, curvaceous creation in Cass Bay, Christchurch, that is as much of an artwork as it is a house

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Staffordshire bull terrier requires attention when it suffers complete paralysis in its back legs, and a Devon rex cat is brought in after being struck by a car. Its eight-year-old owner faces a tough decision when X-rays reveal a severely broken pelvis and a ruptured bladder. Plus, a young cavalier king has had seven operations in a bid to fix its knee joint, and Noel tries to resolve the problem

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: A look back at the transformation of Dick and Angel's 45-room chateau in France from abandoned ruin to sumptuous family home. The series begins by recalling the technical and engineering challenges they had to overcome to make it habitable for their young family. The couple remember their first winter together, when Dick's ingenious solutions were put to the test

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In 2003 Gil and Hillary Briffa decided to retire to southern Spain, where they planned to build a contemporary home in the Andalusian hills against the backdrop of more traditional properties. A few years later, Gil died, but now Kevin McCloud revisits Hillary to find out whether her residence has settled into its landscape and how life is treating her as a British widow living abroad

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary catching up with London's Crossrail engineers less than two years after the project's excavations uncovered the 14th-century Charterhouse plague pit. In an extraordinary turn of events, further work on the Liverpool Street station has revealed another burial ground, this time accommodating the capital's radicals, rebels and outsiders. The Bedlam burial ground, which is thought to be the final resting place of nearly 20,000 people, was believed to be lost for around 300 years. Now, with the excavation process under way, archaeologists and historians have been using bone analysis techniques in conjunction with parish records to reveal the names and life stories of those whose remains were found on-site. Their research reveals that people came from as far afield as Africa to work in the English capital, and sheds light on how pollution first became one of the city's most prolific killers

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The matter of toxic wrecks around the world from the Second World War, as 80 years later vessels are disintegrating and spilling their toxic loads out into the open sea. A sunken oil tanker with a paper-thin hull is on the verge of creating a huge oil slick contaminating Scandinavian beaches, while in the Thames Estuary, an American ammunition ship is a ticking time bomb

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients dealt with at King's College Hospital, London include a man who was stabbed in his own home and has injuries to his lung, arm and liver, and an elderly woman injured in a fall. A former international lawyer who lost his wife, family and home to alcoholism decides to make a change after being treated with care and compassion

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and James Acaster, while Morgana Robinson joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary catching up with London's Crossrail engineers less than two years after the project's excavations uncovered the 14th-century Charterhouse plague pit. In an extraordinary turn of events, further work on the Liverpool Street station has revealed another burial ground, this time accommodating the capital's radicals, rebels and outsiders. The Bedlam burial ground, which is thought to be the final resting place of nearly 20,000 people, was believed to be lost for around 300 years. Now, with the excavation process under way, archaeologists and historians have been using bone analysis techniques in conjunction with parish records to reveal the names and life stories of those whose remains were found on-site. Their research reveals that people came from as far afield as Africa to work in the English capital, and sheds light on how pollution first became one of the city's most prolific killers

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The matter of toxic wrecks around the world from the Second World War, as 80 years later vessels are disintegrating and spilling their toxic loads out into the open sea. A sunken oil tanker with a paper-thin hull is on the verge of creating a huge oil slick contaminating Scandinavian beaches, while in the Thames Estuary, an American ammunition ship is a ticking time bomb

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt visits China to find out how they maximise the chances of finding pearls, and helps to cultivate them from a surprising creature that is definitely not an oyster. Meanwhile, Kate Quilton investigates whether it is safe to eat sliced ham that has developed a weird rainbow pattern, and discovers a bizarre connection between the meat and butterflies

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie teams up with cider-maker Andy Thomstone to make a winter version of a popular drink, and demonstrates how to create the Italian favourite limoncello using leftover lemons

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers try to track down the relatives of a man who became estranged from his family and died without a will, and an heir embarks on a journey of discovery in a bid to understand his late cousin's secret involvement in the Cold War. Plus, more details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Nantwich and Stoke-on-Trent, where Henry comes across a 1960s moped, and Simon restores an early Mirror Dinghy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores a petrol pump and turns a hubcap into a clock, and Simon O'Brien repurposes a copper immersion heater into a stylish wash basin

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend join forces to tackle Tim's very own 1965 Ford Mustang, preparing for a road trip around Britain revisiting a few of their favourite cars from across the last four series. En route, they stop off at some top petrolhead pitstops, and there's a chance to enjoy unseen footage and outtakes from across the series, including a chat with actor Roger Moore

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Steph is trapped with multiple injuries after a bad smash near Alnwick in Northumberland. An accident with a bonfire leaves Paul with serious burns, while in Norfolk's Thetford Forest it's difficult for the medics to reach horse-rider Alice, who has badly broken her ankle

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury. The duo are keen to show off what their B&B is all about, so lead an afternoon of group sound healing. While Ben Davenport and Sian Dewaal from the Fritton Arms immediately recognise that this B&B will be tough competition, Stevie King and his head chef Dave from SugarBeat Eating House dismiss the activity as being 'hippy'. However, it's the poor night's sleep that bothers Kathryn and her best friend Trevor, from Treberthan B&B, as well as the fact they have to cook their own breakfast. At feedback time, the idea of self B&B comes under fire and Kathryn doesn't hold back with her sleep score

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality. Everyone's impressed by the high standard of cleanliness, but Stevie and David are put off by the old-fashioned decor. A Cornish pasty making competition reveals Kathryn's assertive streak, while professional chef David feels under pressure to win, Ben and Sian make an unconventional pasty, and Shannon and Trevor forget a vital ingredient. At breakfast, a request for goat's milk puts Kathryn under pressure

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stevie King and his head chef David Lovett host at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe in Norfolk. The cleanliness comes under fire as Kathryn and Trevor struggle with their marked shower screen, and Ben and Sian write a message in the dust they find. Over dinner, Ben goes into detective mode to find out everything he can about his SugarBeat rivals. However, it's Kathryn's straight talking that becomes the talking point for Shannon and Trevor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Mother-of-six Kelly pursues her dream of recreating a historic villa she fell in love with as a child, although her husband Joe would prefer a more modern house. The family of eight are living together in a two-bedroom barn in Helensville, north west of Auckland, while Kelly and Joe project manage the build

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The look back at the epic transformation of Dick Strawbridge and wife Angel's 45-room chateau continues, focusing on how Angel's creativity brought the chateau back to life, as walls were smashed, exquisitely designed rooms created and stunning bathroom suites installed

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets actor Sean Simons, who has bought the ruins of Cloontykilla Castle in Co Roscommon with a view to using them to create a spectacular mock 16th-century home. However, the outlandish plans - including Jacuzzis in the battlements and water-spouting gargoyles - have Kevin worried the project could become a garish nightmare, and his scepticism increases when he realises Sean has not consulted an architect. Disaster seems to be looming as the contractors grow frustrated and planning regulations are ignored, but somehow things slowly begin to take shape - until a national economic crisis plunges everything into chaos

programme.title: Nordic Murders
programme.desc: When small-time criminal Jens Kampwirth tries to hold up wealthy restauranteur Ingo Wiek, things go pear-shaped and both perpetrator and victim end up dead. Ellen Norgaard finds unexpected connections between them, but also finds out that any investigation on Usedom involves having Karin Lossow as a shadow. Karin is determined to be part of the case, using it to distract herself from her feelings. Detective drama, starring Katrin Sass and Lisa Maria Potthoff. In German

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 77-year-old former motorbike racer arrives with a potentially deadly swelling of the main artery that could burst at any moment. His only option is major surgery, which could be dangerous at his age. A woman whose boyfriend was killed in a cycling accident a year previously is also injured while on her bike, and a medical technician and a mobile caterer talk about their roles in aiding people to recovery

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The medical team faces a range of sport and drink-related injuries on a busy Saturday, including two women hurt when the bar they were dancing on collapsed, and a bricklayer badly cut in an attempted mugging. The night's most dramatic case occurs when a father and son are brought in with life-threatening injuries after being attacked by gatecrashers at a family birthday party, one of whom was armed with a samurai sword

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Rob Beckett take on guest team captain Alan Carr and Sarah Millican in the classic words-and-numbers quiz, while Nick Mohammed joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 77-year-old former motorbike racer arrives with a potentially deadly swelling of the main artery that could burst at any moment. His only option is major surgery, which could be dangerous at his age. A woman whose boyfriend was killed in a cycling accident a year previously is also injured while on her bike, and a medical technician and a mobile caterer talk about their roles in aiding people to recovery

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty wants to know why jelly won't set if chopped papaya is added to it, so he visits South Africa and discovers that the exotic fruit contains enzymes that can also help tenderise meat. In Hawaii, Kate Quilton visits America's only tuna auction to find out why some fish have dangerous levels of mercury, while others don't. She also visits a groundbreaking tech start-up in San Francisco that hopes to feed the world by growing fish flesh in a dish. Matt Tebbutt finds out why homemade ice cubes are cloudy, when the ones bought in shops are clear

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates the sugar tax, and finds out how manufacturers have responded by reducing the sugar in some of our favourite brands, asking what exactly they are adding to keep the flavours the same. Matt Tebbutt is in Italy to find out why strange white stripes are appearing more and more often on chicken breasts, and Kate Quilton asks why some sliced bread falls apart when it's buttered, while other such loaves stay firm

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton show Bev and Gary D'Cruz homes in the Cotswolds and Lanzarote, with a budget of £600,000 for the UK and £400,000 for the island in the Canaries

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show Hertfordshire couple Phil and Geraldine properties in Devon and Bergerac, with a UK budget of £375,000 and £275,000 for France

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton show Neil and Sarah Marks properties in Carcassonne and Wiltshire, as they look to settle down following Neil's retirement

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The patisserie chefs face the quarter-final, and for their first challenge they serve up 24 Black Forest gateaux and 24 mini roulades. For the showpiece, the teams go back to the 1970s as they construct a disco-inspired showpiece, using a combination of sugar and chocolate, and incorporating desserts. Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: Full Bloom
programme.desc: America's budding florists compete in a design contest. In the first round, the contestants must create botanical waterfalls and recreations of Van Gogh paintings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, the first host, company director Sue, promises an evening her guests will never forget. Joining her are business guru Marc, teaching assistant Kim, manager Paul and mole catcher Michael. Sue hopes to get things off to a great start, but when her starter reminds Kim of baby food and Marc of toilets, things begin to look bleak

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, business guru Marc intends to use his coaching skills to plan an impressive night, but after annoying his fellow diners, he has a tough job ahead

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teaching assistant Kim hopes to win over her guests with a summer holiday-themed evening, as she hosts the third dinner party of the week in the West Midlands

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: College manager and part-time comedian Paul is the fourth to play host in the West Midlands, turning his home into a pub in the hope of pulling in the points by pulling a few pints

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the last night in the West Midlands, and mole-catcher Michael intends to impress his guests with his night of fancy French cuisine, including a controversial main course

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Birmingham paramedics try to figure out if a collapsed office worker is having a stroke, while Coventry-based paramedics have the same dilemma with a 76-year-old woman. A self-employed window fitter faces a driver's worst nightmare when he blacks out at the wheel, and a woman has a serious head injury after a fall while ice skating

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The A&E staff of King's College Hospital in London treat patients involved in accidents, including a construction worker who broke his leg in three places and suffered injuries to his chest after being trapped under a cherry picker. Plus, a man who fell from a ladder and landed on his head, and a widower who had a nasty encounter with a mandoline while chopping vegetables

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A retired dockworker who suffers from diabetes is brought in with severe stomach pain and doctors believe part of his gut may have permanently stopped working, creating a life-threatening situation where immediate surgery is the only option. Other patients include a man who has twisted his knee dancing in a club, and a cyclist brought in unconscious after falling off his bike

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson in the words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Craggy Island's most famous religious relic is due to be upgraded and Ted sets about trying to ensure the ceremony runs smoothly. However, Dougal and Jack put his plans in jeopardy when they meet the three priests in charge of proceedings. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal allows himself to be led astray by rebellious new arrival Father Damien, and Ted is suspected of being the criminal mastermind behind a scandalous theft. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Joe Rooney

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The A&E staff of King's College Hospital in London treat patients involved in accidents, including a construction worker who broke his leg in three places and suffered injuries to his chest after being trapped under a cherry picker. Plus, a man who fell from a ladder and landed on his head, and a widower who had a nasty encounter with a mandoline while chopping vegetables

Other patients include a man who has twisted his knee dancing in a club, and a cyclist brought in unconscious after falling off his bike

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Craggy Island's most famous religious relic is due to be upgraded and Ted sets about trying to ensure the ceremony runs smoothly. However, Dougal and Jack put his plans in jeopardy when they meet the three priests in charge of proceedings. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Sin City Motors
programme.desc: Steve Darnell and the crew work on a tatty old vehicle that has been sprung from the desert and looks like something from the Mad Max movies

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike cannot resist making an offer on retro boat and a BMW micro car at a garage in California, and later, he and Frank comb through a huge stockpile at a radiator shop

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Heavy D is in town and is prepared to risk having his fingers burned for the chance of a big score, while auctioneer Sean Kelly keeps a watchful eye

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the hunters are off to Leeds, where a new pair of bidders arrive - and they are not afraid to throw their weight around. Meanwhile, Sam and Ione re-ignite their feud

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank saves a vintage muscle car from meeting a rusty end, while Mike falls for a 100-year-old hotrod. A man cave also presents them with some sweet deals

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Pickers Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz make waves when they hit the road in a one-of-a-kind rocket car

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Customs and Border Protection officers think they have spotted a trend when they seize drugs from behind the dashboards of two cars. A Mexican woman asks officers for protection

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May find the fastest way to get around London - bike, boat or car - in a race through the capital during rush hour. The Stig takes to the test track in the Caparo T1, the Aston Martin V8 Vantage Roadster is pitted against a man with three rockets strapped to his back, and Simon Cowell is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef ends his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures by visiting Bangladesh, sampling the curry on offer in the country that has played a major role in establishing the success of Indian restaurants throughout the UK

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson test-drives two estate cars - the BMW M5 and Mercedes E63. Richard Hammond tries out motor-home racing, while James May pits an Alfa Romeo 159 against a man who crosses the Humber estuary without using its famous bridge. The Stig puts Honda's Civic Type R through its paces and former England rugby union forward Lawrence Dallaglio is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an edition first broadcast in 2007, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May celebrate British Leyland's 40th birthday by trying to boost the reputation of the cars that once rolled off its production line. The Aston Martin DBS is put through its paces on the test track, Jennifer Saunders is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and the team asks whatever happened to the beach buggy

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts the comic improv show, with regulars Wayne Brady, Brad Sherwood, Colin Mochrie and Ryan Stiles

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Actor Ralph Macchio joins in the fun on the classic improv show, with Wayne Brady, Jonathan Mangum, Colin Mochrie and Ryan Stiles.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell feature in a special episode of out-takes, looking back over some of the best moments. Among the celebrity guests are Kevin Bishop, Warwick Davis, Romesh Ranganathan, Hugh Dennis, Sara Cox and Brian Blessed

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry is joined by guests Bill Bailey, Eddie Izzard, Danny Baker and regular panellist Alan Davies on the quiz with a difference. Correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the host asks questions on the topic of house and home, and awards points for the answers he finds the most interesting

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Lou Sanders for a two-day break in the Norwegian city of Bergen, taking a speedboat transfer from the airport to their waterfront lodgings. They explore the historic neighbourhood of Bryggen, ride a cable car to the top of Mt Ulriken and fly down the fastest zipline in Norway - against Richard's better judgement

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and comedian Ellie Taylor head to the Spanish capital for a two-day break, following an itinerary rich in art, history, histrionics and ham. The pair go on a guided tour in which they view masterpieces by Bosch, Velasquez, Picasso and Dali, before they attend a ham-carving workshop and a sample a five-course tasting menu, of which four courses are pork or ham, including a tartare dish. They also take in a lively boating lake, a circus church and a grisly tour of the Spanish Inquisition

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson test-drives two estate cars - the BMW M5 and Mercedes E63. Richard Hammond tries out motor-home racing, while James May pits an Alfa Romeo 159 against a man who crosses the Humber estuary without using its famous bridge. The Stig puts Honda's Civic Type R through its paces and former England rugby union forward Lawrence Dallaglio is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an edition first broadcast in 2007, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May celebrate British Leyland's 40th birthday by trying to boost the reputation of the cars that once rolled off its production line. The Aston Martin DBS is put through its paces on the test track, Jennifer Saunders is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and the team asks whatever happened to the beach buggy

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef retraces a road trip he took in 1968 down the Pacific Coast Highway to the Mexican border and beyond. He begins in San Francisco, the home of sourdough and where America's love affair with seasonal cooking took hold through restaurants like Chez Panisse, run by Alice Waters, who Rick is keen to meet. The chef also reflects on the time he had his first taste of Mexican food

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motor racing driver Lewis Hamilton demonstrates his abilities on the test track, and Richard Hammond tries his hand in a Formula One car. Jeremy Clarkson and James May assess historic vehicles to establish which one pioneered the control layout used in modern cars, and singer-songwriter James Blunt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: QI

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Offbeat tourism show in which Richard Ayoade is joined by celebrity guests to enjoy the most efficient weekend break possible. He begins in Barcelona with comedienne and actress Kathy Burke, experiencing the best of this popular holiday destination in just 48 hours. The pair sample adventurous cuisine, including potatoes coated in edible clay, and a cava tasting session quickly descends into disarray. Other highlights include a visit to the Barcelona Football Club museum, a guided tricycle tour, and a trip to see the surrealist work of Catalan artist Joan Miro

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by fellow comedian Adam Hills on a whirlwind weekend in Turkey's largest city. He wants to show The Last Leg host how to holiday with ruthless efficiency, as they cross the River Bosphorus from Europe to Asia, comparing a private yacht tour with the low-price public ferry. The pair have an authentic Turkish shave at a local barbershop that turns out to be a lot closer than they were expecting, and share an awkward moment at a local bath and massage parlour. There's also a visit to the Grand Bazaar market, where both men have a go at haggling

programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Cumbria, where he visits the Sellafield site that reprocesses fuel from nuclear reactors, drinks a Victorian brew made from the waters of Cockermouth and explores a Japanese-inspired garden created in the 19th century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins his journey in Kirkby-in-Furness, Cumbria, where he learns about the village's famous blue slates. He then heads to Barrow to examine the secret world of nuclear submarines and finishes his trip in Lancaster, where he discovers why executions at the castle drew so many spectators in Victorian times

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Piece Hall in Halifax, the only surviving 18th-century cloth hall where textiles were once traded. Items featured include a sapphire that jewellery specialist John Benjamin thinks is the biggest he has ever seen - but which the owner thinks is fake. Other finds include a Chinese imperial robe, a mysterious set of 17th-century doors, and the oldest piece of glass to ever appear on the show

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: How China has developed one of the world's fastest high speed rail networks, allowing 75 million people across the country to reach Shanghai within an hour

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Unexplained events during wartime, including German submarines that discovered previously unknown sea creatures and a Japanese bomb that floated on the jet stream

programme.title: Churchill's Bodyguard
programme.desc: The Nazis launch another attempt on Churchill's life, with Hitler authorising a mission by a suicide parachute squad. programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A volunteer is tasked with restoring a legendary Le Mans racer. A decommissioned bomb proves difficult, and can the experts revive the dormant engines of a transatlantic flyer?

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: At the South Devon Railway, Andy Leech and Liz Turner host afternoon tea for 44 passengers. In East Lancashire, volunteer Chandra Law takes on the new role of Stationmaster

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the Co Meath town of Enfield to the east coast port of Drogheda in Co Louth. He explores the railway network within the Bog of Allen, learns about the Victorian secrets of the Boyne Viaduct, and ventures into underground zinc mines

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Victorians' fascination with antiquity as he continues his tour of the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland. He visits the cromlech stones of Dundalk, Co Louth, and an observatory in Co Armagh, and travels on the steam railway in Downpatrick, Co Down

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to London's West End as he explores 19th-century engineering used in theatres, and learns about the ambitious building programme that saw Trafalgar Square replace slums. He indulges in some retail therapy at a Piccadilly Circus perfumery patronised by kings, queens and prime ministers, and finds out how diseases were rife in Victorian London on a visit to Soho

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on his final journey in London, exploring Albertopolis in south Kensington and reaching dizzying heights inside a Victorian landmark. At Vauxhall, he learns about the darker side of London's flower market in Bradshaw's day and ends at London Bridge, where two stations are becoming one and a new concourse is being built

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from the heart of the industrial East Midlands to the island of Lindisfarne off the coast of Northumberland. On the first leg, he gives an old engine a fresh start in the railway hub of Derby, heads to Nottingham to find out about the Victorian origins of a well-known high-street chemist and calls at a historic one-time grocer's shop in Grantham, Lincolnshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, with this leg beginning in Boston, south Lincolnshire, where he explores the connection between the town and its American namesake. At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, he discovers the origins of a favourite Victorian apple before heading into West Yorkshire to visit an imposing institution built in Menston to provide asylum for those with mental health issues. In nearby Wakefield, Michael boards one of Britain's least frequent services, before heading to Hensall, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in the East Yorkshire town of Hessle, where he learns about the technology that made the Humber Bridge possible. In nearby Hull, Michael hears from local MP Alan Johnson about anti-slavery campaigner William Wilberforce, before travelling to Scarborough Castle, where the founder of the Quaker movement was once imprisoned. His last stop of the day is York, where he learns what made the city a centre for the sweet-making industry

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins the fourth leg of his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne in Middlesbrough, visiting one of the last iron foundries in the town and helping cast a carrot valve for a steam engine. His next stop is Darlington, Co Durham, where he meets the editor of the Northern Echo and finds out about the colourful history of WT Stead, one of his predecessors. Michael then heads to St Paul's monastery in Jarrow, Tyne and Wear, to learn about eighth-century monk the Venerable Bede, known as 'the father of English history', before finishing in Hexham, Northumberland, where he visits a historic ginger beer emporium

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Newcastle on the final leg of his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, finding out about the world's earliest swing bridge and its inventor William Armstrong. He also travels by boat from the port of Seahouses to examine the story of lighthouse keeper's daughter Grace Darling, who, along with her father, rescued nine people from tumultuous seas in 1838. On the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, Michael explores the lime kilns and finds out how Christianity spread from here across northern England in the 7th century

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from south-west Wales to Cambridge, beginning in Pembroke Dock, where Queen Victoria's royal yachts were built. In the little market town of Naberth he investigates what caused rebels to dress up as women, later spending the night at an inn in Carmarthen where Horatio Nelson once met Emma Hamilton. Next day, the former politician pitches in with the volunteers who look after the Gwili Heritage line and ends his leg in Swansea, where he learns how to pose for a photograph in Victorian style

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Domestic disharmony ensues for the Meldrews as a routine trip to the garden centre starts a trail of turmoil including a scowling snowman, a laughing policeman and a mysterious saboteur. Christmas edition of David Renwick's comedy from 1994, starring Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Angus Deayton

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Patrick's plans for a quiet birthday with Pippa are left in tatters when the Meldrews join them for a meal at a Chinese restaurant. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor's unease at the possible outcome of a medical test is compounded by the discovery that he has just bought a car with an obscene number plate. programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Radio expert Mark Stuckey has a job on his hands, repairing a 1930s transistor radio brought into the Repair Shop by David Hill from Brecon. The radio was given to David's father by an army officer on the beaches at Dunkirk during the Second World War, and after being successfully shepherded through the bullets and the crashing waves to safety, it became the Hill family's pride and joy. programme.desc: Web designer Marc Harris hosts the penultimate dinner party in Gloucestershire. He is confident his Spanish menu will win over his fellow diners and is determined to put a halt to Zac's rude behaviour, but his plans go downhill when he makes a mess of his starter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Council worker Ameena Cott hosts the final dinner party in Gloucestershire, and hopes to impress with a glamorous evening, which includes colour co-ordinating her dress to match her dog's outfit and making a birdcage centrepiece from a melon. She also tries to win her guests' affections with electric pulse-pad treatment and strawberry face masks, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Mike & Nellie's Steakhouse in Oakhurst, New Jersey, where owner Mike Farber is mourning his father Nellie, who was also his business partner. Having been called in by the restaurateur's two daughters, Gordon faces the task of getting him to focus on his business, before tackling the bigger problem of rotting decor and a less-than-appealing menu

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Guest-house owners in Spain hope their music event will not attract the local police. In France, a motorhome rental business deals with damaged vehicles and the owners of a holiday village in Portugal deal with an unfortunate leak

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Alison and her daughter Paula are looking for a holiday home that feels traditionally Spanish. Presenter Jasmine Harman shows them five properties around Velez-Malaga for their budget of £60,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ex-police officer Steve McGibbon is looking for the perfect holiday home in Fuerteventura where he can spend quality time with his daughter Madison. With his stepmother Sandra on hand for advice and five options lined up by property expert Ben Hillman for his budget of up to £120,000, he hopes his long list of exacting requirements will be met

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at some of their favourite properties from the show. They begin in Cullercoats, on North Tyneside, where Mark and Suze Drummond have emotional ties to their Victorian townhouse - special memories that are making it difficult for Suzie to consider leaving. In Rushden, Northamptonshire, Susan and John Antoniou-Bailey are torn over their unique five-bedroom home. Susan loves its history and original features, but John has had enough of constantly moving between the disjointed, boxy rooms

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A rugby player is scanned for spinal damage following a collision during a match, and describes her struggles with mental health issues as she awaits the results. A teacher is knocked off his bicycle after receiving some bad news, while a man who broke two bones in his ankle after a fall is assisted by his dead wife's best friend

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman with brittle bone disease who is in agony after a fall - only to find themselves with two patients on their hands as her daughter collapses in front of them. A young man whose blood sugar levels are so high is in danger of slipping into a diabetic coma, while a middle-aged man with a history of heart problems refuses to go to hospital

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Daisy May Cooper take on guest team captain Richard Ayoade and Ivo Graham. Adam Buxton is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, and Joe Wilkinson joins Rachel Riley at the numbers board

programme.title: Kathy Burke: Money Talks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kathy explores the idea of escaping poverty through hard work, and considers the unseen obstacles that make this kind of social change difficult. She visits one of the most deprived towns in Britain and meets a local man trying to build community spirit, and discovers a community in Somerset for people who have rejected capitalism

programme.title: Can I Improve My Memory?
programme.desc: Memory techniques and hacks are employed to help celebrities learn as much as they can about a subject that they know absolutely nothing about. In this first programme, boxing champion Chris Eubank is tasked with memorising hundreds of facts about Jurassic dinosaurs, Strictly Come Dancing legend Len Goodman is given '90s American hip-hop to master, Love Island winner Amber Gill is assigned British birds, Naked Attraction presenter Anna Richardson is tasked with the solar system, while actress Nina Wadia must memorise the human skeleton. Hosted by Sandi Toksvig

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits professional deep-sea diver Adrian Corrigall and his wife Megan, who planned to build their new family home in rural East Sussex almost entirely out of concrete, with construction involving cutting-edge technologies conceived in Switzerland and never used to build a house before. With both the schedule and the budget under strain, Adrian was forced to resume work as a North Sea diver, leaving Megan juggling this messy and challenging build, all the while looking after three young children. By 2018, water leaks were still to be addressed, the majority of the spaces were raw and unfinished, and the family were still a long way away from moving into their concrete dream. Two and a half years later, with the house now fully complete, Kevin returns to see what living in a cutting-edge concrete bunker is really like

programme.title: Scotland's Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels the beautiful coastal railways of Scotland, and meets the vibrant characters who live and work along its shoreline. In North Berwick she chats to Jane McMinn, IT consultant-turned-master mariner and lobster breeder. Other destinations include Edinburgh, where Julie discovers the intriguing connections between the railway and the famous Balmoral Hotel's Palm Court and Clock Tower

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the B&B owners meet for the final time, things get heated when Martin and Kyle want to know why Jean and Dec gave their dressing gowns a thumbs down. Mary and Julian are keen to understand why the price wasn't right for Jean and Dec, while Kerry and Andy want to know why they got a low hosting score

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples in Greater Manchester compete in the culinary challenge - caravan enthusiasts Jane and Colin, party-loving Rachel and Dave, and Martyn and Russell who host a night with sparkle. In order to win the £1,000 prize some of the hosts pull out all the stops - with desserts even arriving via zip wire

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Clare are looking for a holiday home in central Portugal, with Paul's passion for biking a key part of their search. Jasmine Harman sets out to find a place that can double up as a bikers' retreat, on their £120,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sharon Taylor and John Healey both have busy teaching jobs in London, and long to spend a bit more quality time together. After several trips to Spain they have decided they want to buy a holiday home on Spain's Costa Almeria. With a budget of £75,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties, from a townhouse with stunning sea views to a brand new beach apartment, but will any of them hit the mark?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Farzana Kausar from Stockport, Greater Manchester, is looking for a better quality of life in the Spanish sun. Her daughter Aisha, who uses a wheelchair, is also making the full-time move. With a budget of £120,000 for the property and any alterations they'll need to make for Aisha, Laura Hamilton shows them five homes around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shop manager Nicola Steele hosts the first dinner party in Cardiff, and hopes that her dog-themed surprises and unusual entertainment will impress her guests - army wife Rachael Smith, casting agency owner Jerry Lockett, childminder Andrea Grainger and chatterbox Sam Clark - and win her the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jerry Lockett hosts the second dinner party in Cardiff, and following a flood at his home, hopes to impress his guests by serving the meal at the grand venue of his cousin's mansion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Army wife Rachael Smith hosts the third party in Cardiff, allowing her competitive edge and no-nonsense approach to come to the fore in her bid to pick up the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Costume designer Sam Clarke prepares a Mexican menu for the fourth dinner party in Cardiff. Everything does not go to plan, however, when two guests begin fighting at the table, and his 'fun' game leaves everyone in tears

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Childminder Andrea Grainger hosts the last dinner party as the competition in Cardiff draws to a close. She hopes a menu of traditional Welsh dishes will help her secure the prize, and lines up a selection of games to improve her chances of success. As the evening comes to an end, the diners discover which of the meals made the best impression

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family-run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the season draws to a close, the Brits abroad reflect on a strange summer. In Spain, a guest house tries to tempt Spanish visitors with local cuisine and a cycling retreat wins some much-needed business, while a Portuguese holiday village takes delivery of a massive new centrepiece

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ex-police officer Steve McGibbon is looking for the perfect holiday home in Fuerteventura where he can spend quality time with his daughter Madison. With his stepmother Sandra on hand for advice and five options lined up by property expert Ben Hillman for his budget of up to £120,000, he hopes his long list of exacting requirements will be met

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a couple with a steadily expanding brood that has rapidly outgrown their narrowboat home. The pair are reluctant to give up their river-based lifestyle, and are on the market for a much wider Dutch barge. However, following through with their plan would mean sacrificing room height, leading to the development of a radical solution. He also visits a family planning to transform a dilapidated swimming pool into a sunken garden room, and heads to a Second World War decontamination bunker that has found a new lease of life as a luxury dwelling. Plus, the presenter's German road trip continues as he take in a futuristic lakeside house with sliding doors that provide an impressive view

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George follows the progress of a couple who want to transform two dilapidated air-raid shelters in their garden into his and hers sheds - but are arguing over who gets which one. He also meets the youngest designer ever featured on the show. Twelve-year-old Guinnie is taking her school project, to design the ultimate party shed, off the page and into her back garden - with the help of her builder dad. Will Hardie continues work on the floating beach hut and visits a million-pound beach house, to find out what is possible if money is no object, and in Germany, George explores an old water tower that has been transformed into a luxury home

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Police officers in Northamptonshire respond to an ever increasing number of 999 calls concerning people living in shared accommodation. The number of houses of multiple occupancy (HMO) has nearly trebled since 2013. PC Sean Whelan is dispatched to one HMO to deal with a fight in the shared kitchen. He also has to mediate between a householder who hasn't been paid the rent he's owed and a tenant with no contract in place. PC Debbie Wishart attends a hostel where she arrests a woman suspected of stealing a chicken Kiev, while PC Sam Clulow attends a call involving a vulnerable heroin user who's being preyed upon

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A rugby player is scanned for spinal damage following a collision during a match, and describes her struggles with mental health issues as she awaits the results. A teacher is knocked off his bicycle after receiving some bad news, while a man who broke two bones in his ankle after a fall is assisted by his dead wife's best friend

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise Van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, and Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr assess the week's biggest news stories, hard-hitting documentaries, laughable comedies and gritty dramas

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The patisserie chefs face the quarter-final, and for their first challenge they serve up 24 Black Forest gateaux and 24 mini roulades. For the showpiece, the teams go back to the 1970s as they construct a disco-inspired showpiece, using a combination of sugar and chocolate, and incorporating desserts. Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: This Way Up
programme.desc: Aine's excitement mounts as she prepares for a big date with Richard, while the new business venture that sister Shona set up with Charlotte gets off to an awkward start. The return of the comedy, written by and starring Aisling Bea, with Sharon Horgan, Tobias Menzies, Indira Varma and Aasif Mandvi

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Seawood Hotel in Lynton, Devon, where owners Martin and Kyle Fletcher hope to win over their guests with their high standards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Gooseford Farm B&B in Dartmoor, Devon, where Mary Courtier looks after the B&B and husband Julian takes care of the farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Trevarrian Lodge in Newquay, Cornwall, where Jean Hegarty and her husband - professional chef Dec - are hoping the food will win their guests over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Rivendell Outdoor Club, a clothing-optional resort in Wimborne, Dorset, managed by couple Kerry Murphy and Andy Gautry. On arrival not all the contestants are happy to let it all hang out but a warm welcome from the hosts soon puts them all at ease

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the B&B owners meet for the final time, things get heated when Martin and Kyle want to know why Jean and Dec gave their dressing gowns a thumbs down. Mary and Julian are keen to understand why the price wasn't right for Jean and Dec, while Kerry and Andy want to know why they got a low hosting score

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples host dinner parties in east London, competing for the cash prize of £1,000. Newlyweds Anita and Terry plan to recreate their wedding day, complete with menu and speeches. Night two sees competitive foodies Flora and Chris put on a restaurant-inspired meal, including a surprise amuse-bouche. The third evening sees proud east Londoners Cecelia and Paul do their best to mellow the spices

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first hosts in north London, recently engaged couple Anita and Cash, serve up a menu that draws on their Indian heritage. On day two, laughter-loving Keith and his laid-back wife Maz dish out good, honest food, but with foodie guests judging them, they're a bit worried. Dan and Les, who've been married for 17 years, host last. Self-confessed food snob Dan has devised a starter with eight separate elements. Will their risky choices and haute cuisine appeal to their guests?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Young Brighton couple Hannah and Luke kick off the competition with an ambitiously trendy menu, then more experienced hosts Karen and Mark step up to the plate the next day, followed by Rachel and TJ, who have given nothing away to their fellow hosts

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lecturer Maxine and paramedic team leader Cormac got married in the scenic northern region of the Dordogne in France, and now want Ben Hillman's help to find them their dream holiday home there. They have a budget of just over £100,000 and would ideally like a property with lots of outside space, just a short walk from the town of Riberac.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lynn and Gerald are looking for a holiday home in the north of Spain's Murcia region, where they can enjoy the Mediterranean sunshine and numerous golf courses the area has to offer. Ben Hillman lines up five options iwthin their budget of £100,000, hoping at least one will encourage the pair to make an offer

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: House-hunters Gill and Mark from south Wales want to move to the French countryside of Charente, so Ben HIllman shows them five properties for their budget of £110,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Prison officer Cecil and foster carer Patricia have a budget of up to £180,000 and want to buy a holiday home in Vero Beach, Florida. Scarlette Douglas shows them a few options

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Glenys from Solihull find her perfect holiday in La Mata on Spain's Costa Blanca. Glenys has been searching for the perfect location for several years, but when she visited La Mata, she knew it was the right place for her. With a budget of £140,000, she's ready to make her dream a reality, but it's a big decision so she's bringing friend Martine along for support

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jill and Paul Phillips hope to move to a smallholding and lead a self-sufficient life in Brittany, north-west France, so Danni Menzies shows them five properties for their budget of £135,000 hoping to find the right home for them

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Phil Spencer returns to Cullercoats on North Tyneside to catch up with Mark and Suze, who were unable to agree on what to do with their four-bed Victorian town house. When Kirstie and Phil first met the couple nine months earlier, emotional ties to their home had left them in limbo, with Suzie feeling like she was unable to leave, but Mark longed to move into a bigger home with a better layout for their family. Now, Phil returns to find out if they followed through with their decision

Two and a half years later, with the house now fully complete, Kevin returns to see what living in a cutting-edge concrete bunker is really like
